question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his assessment of the effectiveness of anti-bullying legislation.
answer_text: The Addressing Bullying in Schools Act (Northern Ireland) 2016 came into effect in September 2021, and it requires schools to record incidents or alleged incidents of bullying type behaviour, their motivation and their outcome.  Each school’s Board of Governors (BoG) has responsibility to oversee the implementation of the Act in their school.   The Act places a statutory duty on a BoG collectively to take responsibility for the development, implementation, monitoring and periodic review of the school’s Addressing Bullying policies and procedures.  The Addressing Bullying in Schools Implementation Team (ABSIT) within the Education Authority (EA) was established in September 2022 and provides support for schools, educational settings, young people and parents to help them understand the requirements of the Act.The Department has worked closely with the EA’s Addressing Bullying in Schools Implementation Team (ABSIT) who provide the Department with termly reports in relation to the referrals they deal with, the training they undertake, and the associated outcomes for both. The latest data in relation to ABSIT’s three tier training provided to schools indicates agreement from 90% plus of those who have undertaken the training that, where appropriate across the three tiers, the training has resulted in the following impacts in relation to bullying type behaviour for those who have completed the training:  increased capacity to address bullying type concerns more effectively;  better understanding of statutory requirements; more able to implement systems and processes in relation to bullying type behaviour;  more awareness of effective approaches in relation to bullying type behaviour; more able to lead school systems and processes; more able to map whole school preventative approaches; and more able to lead the selection and implementation of effective responses.The recently published School Omnibus survey also contains data relating to schools’ understanding of the Act and its outworkings, which indicate a high level of compliance and impact within schools. For instance:  97.7% of schools who responded were aware of the duties the Act places on their school.  A similar pattern was found for the awareness of schools Boards of Governors.All school who responded stated that they had implemented the legislative definition of bullying and have an anti-bullying policy with preventative measures updated at intervals of no more than four years.Almost all schools (96.4%) had implemented the statutory duty that Governors must ensure the Anti-Bullying policy is properly implemented and preventative measures kept under review so that it is fit for purpose, and 92.3% had implemented the statutory duty to engage with pupils, parents/carers and the school community when developing and reviewing the anti-bullying policy. Almost all schools (96.8%) had implemented the statutory duty to record incidents of bullying and alleged bullying behaviours including the motivati

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline the options available to Executive Ministers who seek to progress legislation on matters where statutory responsibilities rest with more than one Executive Department.
answer_text: Such Bills would be considered on an individual basis, taking into account the nature and effect of the relevant provisions. However, a Bill of this kind would normally be the subject of cross departmental engagement throughout the course of its development.All Bills require Executive approval before they can be introduced to the Assembly, and this is the case irrespective of the provisions included.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline what support is available to staff in schools to help them address sectarian bullying.
answer_text: The Addressing Bullying in Schools Act (Northern Ireland) 2016 (the Act), requires schools to record incidents of bullying type behaviour, their motivation, methods used and their outcome. question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline how much of his 2025-26 budget will be spent on the Special Educational Needs Reform Agenda and five-year Delivery Plan.
answer_text: A budget of £7.5m has been ring-fenced in 2025/26 for the delivery of the Special Educational Needs (SEN) Reform Agenda and Delivery Plan, which is the year two allocation from my department’s successful bid of £27.5m from the Public Sector Transformation Fund (PSTF).In addition, £3m has been ring-fenced from the Early Learning and Childcare (ELC) Strategy funding to support the delivery of the Early Years actions within the Delivery Plan, including continuation of the Pre-School Education Inclusion Fund (PSEIF) in 2025/26.I have also allocated £70m to the SEN capital investment programme in 2025/26.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline the action he has taken to attract more staff to the early learning and childcare sector.
answer_text: A workforce plan for the early learning and childcare (ELC) sector, including community and voluntary providers, has been identified by stakeholders as a priority and will be included within the draft ELC Strategy I plan to bring forward for Executive agreement and public consultation in autumn 2025. As part of the ELC measures delivered in 2024-25, I provided funding to the Department of Communities to provide childminding academies through Labour Market Partnerships, which resulted in an additional 146 childminders joining the sector.  This funding will continue in 2025-26.  Further actions to support the recruitment and retention of staff are being considered by the cross-departmental Task and Finish group as part of the development of the overall Strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail if the Independent Review of Assessment Panel will advise on the impact of the current post-primary transfer arrangements on assessment at the end of Key Stage 2.
answer_text: Post-primary transfer assessments are not part of the Review remit. The Independent Review of Assessment Panel will conduct an evidence-based review of assessment approaches internationally and make a series of policy proposals about the purpose, nature and design of state assessment in Northern Ireland.As outlined in the published Terms of Reference one of the objectives of the review is to evaluate the overall coherence of current assessment arrangements across all Key Stages including the transition of assessment data from the primary to post-primary phases.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 26360/22-27.
To ask the Minister of Education to detail any plans he has to develop a workforce strategy for the community and voluntary childcare sector.
answer_text: A workforce plan for the early learning and childcare (ELC) sector, including community and voluntary providers, has been identified by stakeholders as a priority and will be included within the draft ELC Strategy I plan to bring forward for Executive agreement and public consultation in autumn 2025. As part of the ELC measures delivered in 2024-25, I provided funding to the Department of Communities to provide childminding academies through Labour Market Partnerships, which resulted in an additional 146 childminders joining the sector.  This funding will continue in 2025-26.  Further actions to support the recruitment and retention of staff are being considered by the cross-departmental Task and Finish group as part of the development of the overall Strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline the action he has taken to stabilise and support the community and voluntary childcare sector.
answer_text: In 2024/25 I invested almost £10m of the £25m budget for early learning and childcare provision in stabilising and enhancing early years programmes, including those delivered by the community and voluntary sector.  These programmes included Sure Start, the Pathway Fund and Toybox Fund.  I commenced a major expansion of pre-school education which included additional funding for non-statutory pre-school providers participating in the PSEP, providing a one-off non-consolidated payment of £100 per pupil to all non-statutory settings and increasing the funding rate for these providers. This has now been increased for the 2025-26 academic year.I also provided funding to the Department of Health to support the community and voluntary sector through enhanced training opportunities and expansion of the Fair Play Grant Scheme, as well as continuing to fund social economy providers who were delivering the Bright Start School Age Childcare Grant Scheme. Additional investment in these early years programmes will continue in 2025-26 through the £55m ringfenced budget for early learning and childcare provision.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail his assessment of the current governance support arrangements in place for community and voluntary childcare providers.
answer_text: The governance requirements for the early years sector, including community and voluntary childcare providers, are set out in the Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 Volume 2 - Family Support, Child Minding and Day Care Centre alongside the guidance provided by the Minimum Standards for Childminding and Daycare (DoH). The regulation of these requirements in early years services is the responsibility of the Health and Social Care Trusts through registration and inspection processes.Early years services can receive additional support to meet their governance requirements through membership organisations such as The Early Years Organisation, PlayBoard, NI Childminding Association and Altram. Membership organisations receive funding from my Department and/or the Department of Health to provide this support. Community and voluntary providers delivering early learning and childcare programmes funded by my department, receive governance support from the delivery agents for each of the programmes.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 26361/22-27.

To ask the Minister of Education to outline his assessment of the need to provide additional funding to community and voluntary childcare providers who support children and families in disadvantaged areas.
answer_text: The Department for the Economy (DfE) is leading on a scoping exercise to develop a clearer picture of the business support requirements of the whole childcare sector, including community and voluntary provision, to help inform an appropriate government response. I understand the report is currently being finalised.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education if the targeted business support scheme for childcare providers will give particular consideration to the role of the community and voluntary childcare sector.
answer_text: The Department for the Economy (DfE) is leading on a scoping exercise to develop a clearer picture of the business support requirements of the whole childcare sector, including community and voluntary provision, to help inform an appropriate government response. I understand the report is currently being finalised.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline any work he has undertaken with the Minister for Communities to better align community and voluntary organisations supporting children and families in areas of disadvantage.
answer_text: My officials continue to develop the RAISE Programme in collaboration with stakeholders in other government departments, including the Department for Communities.  A Locality Reference Group has been established in each RAISE locality involving community and voluntary organisations, local government, schools and others with valuable roles to play in supporting children’s learning in the locality.  My officials are also represented on the NICS Collaboration Test and Learn Operational Steering Group led by the Department for Communities and The Executive Office.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he intends to review the role of the Registered Person in community and voluntary childcare settings.
answer_text: My Department is currently carrying out a review of the Minimum Standards for Childminding and Day Care.  The first stage of the review, a public consultation on adult-child ratios and the requirement for all prospective childcare workers to provide a health declaration countersigned by their GP, will close on 20 June 2025.  A Review Reference Group will then be established to consider the wider Minimum Standards in detail and assist with the drafting of a redesigned set of Standards for consultation early in 2026. Any concerns or issues regarding the role of the Registered Person in community and voluntary childcare settings can be raised through that process.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline (i) his assessment of the impact of inflammatory bowel disease on education; (ii) what support is available to pupils with Crohn's disease and Colitis who may need extended time off school; and (iii) how these students are supported to complete their education.
answer_text: (i)  The department does not hold any data on the number of children in school impacted by the conditions referenced.  These conditions are not medical categories for inclusion on a school’s medical register.  (ii) & (iii) The Education Authority (EA) is committed to meeting the needs of our young people. This is reflected in the EA’s mission statement and in the Strategic Plan which aims at providing our children with the best possible start in life in order that they can fully realise their potential. The EA has a duty to assist schools by putting arrangements in place to support the education of those children who through medical circumstances are unable to engage with school. The Exceptional Teaching Arrangements (ETA) service delivers a high quality service to young people with medical needs in order to provide them with a continuity of education in core subjects and to facilitate a return to their registered schools as soon as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 26364/22-27.
To ask the Minister of Education to detail his assessment of the availability and accessibility of toilets in schools for pupils who have conditions such as Crohn's disease and Colitis.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) is continuously recording and reviewing the condition of the toilet facilities across the estate via their condition surveying programme, whereby issues of an immediate health and safety concern are reported to the Maintenance Helpdesk for remedial action as response maintenance. Unfortunately, due to the constrained capital budget position, there has not been a planned programme of toilet refurbishment works. However, critical health and safety issues will be progressed.Should a school have a concern about the toilets being used by pupils with conditions such as Crohn’s disease and colitis, they should submit an Unavoidable Minor Works Application to either the EA, for controlled schools, or to the Department’s Estate Operations Team for non-controlled schools.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any steps he is taking to improve standards for toilets in schools, so pupils with conditions such as Crohn's disease and Colitis have better access to clean, private and accessible facilities.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) is continuously recording and reviewing the condition of the toilet facilities across the estate via their condition surveying programme, whereby issues of an immediate health and safety concern are reported to the Maintenance Helpdesk for remedial action as response maintenance. Unfortunately, due to the constrained capital budget position, there has not been a planned programme of toilet refurbishment works. However, critical health and safety issues will be progressed.Should a school have a concern about the toilets being used by pupils with conditions such as Crohn’s disease and colitis, they should submit an Unavoidable Minor Works Application to either the EA, for controlled schools, or to the Department’s Estate Operations Team for non-controlled schools.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when he will answer AQW 27512/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 27512/22-27 was answered on 27 June 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to outline what actions he is taking to promote the benefits of early reading.
answer_text: Health visitors, through the core delivery of the Healthy Child Healthy Future programme, encourage parents to interact with their baby through talking, singing and reading books.  The Department of Education, through the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy, fund the Bookstart programme.  Health visitors distribute these book packs to families with young children and also promote the use of local libraries.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to outline what actions he is taking to promote the benefits of early reading.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Libraries NI.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:‘Libraries NI’s Reading and Reader Development Strategy 2024-27 aims to foster a love of reading, to promote the benefits of reading and enhance the reading experience for people of all ages.Libraries NI promotes the benefits of early reading through outreach work such as class visits and collaborative working with the Department of Education.  There are also a number of initiatives delivered by Libraries NI including:Libraries NI Learning Hub and Kids Hub School Preparedness webpageRhythm and RhymeStoryteller in Residence and StorytimeThe Big Summer Read’

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to outline his next steps following the UK Government’s Spending Review 2025.
answer_text: Last week’s Spending Review set a three-year funding envelope for Resource covering the period 2026–2029, and a four-year funding envelope for Capital covering the period 2026–2030. This will allow the Executive to set a multi-year Budget in line with the Spending Review.It is my intention, subject to Executive agreement, to publish a draft multi-year Budget by early December.  This will allow for a twelve-week consultation period before a final Budget is agreed by the Executive prior to the start of the new financial year.Discussions on the long-term work required for a full Fiscal Framework will begin immediately. This cover areas including the borrowing powers of the Housing Executive, the Holtham Review of the Executive’s relative need and fiscal devolution.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail what plans he has to provide investment to support the delivery of community-led housing.
answer_text: I have no current plans for investment in community-led housing.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any legal powers the Education Authority has to direct the placement of a child with a statement of special educational need in a given school.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised that:The placement of children with a Statement of Special Educational Needs (SEN) is in accordance with the legislative requirements provided for by the Education and Libraries (Northern Ireland) Order 1986 and the Education (Northern Ireland) Order 1996.  Article 44 of the Education and Libraries (Northern Ireland) Order 1986 sets out the general principle that:    ‘so far as is compatible with the provision of efficient instruction and training and the avoidance of unreasonable public expenditure, pupils shall be educated in accordance with the wishes of their parents’.   Paragraph 5 of Schedule 2 to the Education (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 provides as follows: Where (the authority) makes a statement in a case where the parent of the child concerned has expressed a preference in pursuance of such arrangements as to the grant-aided school at which he wishes education to be provided for his child, the (authority) shall specify the name of that school in the statement unless:  The school is unsuitable to the child’s age, ability or aptitude or to his special educational needs, or,  The attendance of the child at the school would be incompatible with the provision of efficient education for the children with whom he would be educated or the efficient use of resources. The Education Authority must name the parents’ preferred school in Part 4 of the statement unless there is clear evidence that there are no reasonable steps that could be taken to prevent such an incompatibility.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education if he has considered introducing Mindfulness as part of the Emotional Health and Wellbeing curriculum.
answer_text: The current Northern Ireland Curriculum emphasises the development of the whole child including focusing on emotional wellbeing.  While Mindfulness is not a statutory requirement, schools have the flexibility to teach it as a tool to support pupil wellbeing within the existing curriculum framework. At primary level, Mindfulness can be integrated into the statutory Personal Development and Mutual Understanding area of learning.  At post-primary level, it can be incorporated into the Personal Development strand of Learning for Life and Work.The Council for the Curriculum, Examinations and Assessment (CCEA) has also created a Wellbeing Hub to show how delivery of the statutory requirements of the Northern Ireland Curriculum, and CCEA qualifications, can support emotional wellbeing.  The Hub contains CCEA guidance and links to other relevant support from the Education Authority, the Public Health Agency and other organisations.As you may be aware, the Strategic Review of the Northern Ireland Curriculum was published on 3 June 2025.  It recommends that my Department develops a new curriculum framework for Northern Ireland which has greater specificity about the important knowledge, concepts and skills that children should learn at each stage of their education.  A key recommendation in the report is the establishment of a Taskforce to oversee the design and development of the new curriculum framework.  I have committed to establishing this Taskforce by Autumn 2025.  The Taskforce will work collaboratively to translate the Review’s recommendations into a practical, ambitious framework that reflects our shared vision for education.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline what actions he is taking to promote the benefits of early reading.
answer_text: The importance of reading is promoted throughout the Sure Start programme which my department funds. Sure Start projects provide a wide range of support and resources to promote reading, child development and parental involvement with their child’s early learning. In addition, my department strongly encourages links between Sure Start and Libraries, working in partnership with parents and families to encourage an ongoing relationship with local libraries which is sustained beyond the age of four when children transition from Sure Start.My department also funds the Getting Ready to Learn programme in pre-school settings, which includes the Big Bedtime Read.  This focusses on raising parents' awareness of the importance of reading to children and on encouraging them to read regularly to their children each night. It includes an emphasis on establishing good bedtime routines and on maximizing opportunities throughout the day to engage children in early reading experiences.I have also recently reintroduced the BookStart Baby Programme to Northern Ireland.  This programme encourages a love of reading from an early age to ensure all children get the full benefits of building confidence and developing empathy, creativity, and social skills.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the number of children with a statement of special educational need awaiting a school placement for the start of the 2025-26 academic year.
answer_text: Climate change and sustainable development are topics which are already covered at every level within the current Northern Ireland Curriculum.In addition, in November 2023, my Department commissioned the Council for the Curriculum, Examinations and Assessment (CCEA) to greater embed sustainability and climate change education into the current curriculum by undertaking a Climate Change and Green Growth Project.  Key achievements to date include:A Climate Change Expert Group has been established comprising experts and stakeholders from the Department of Education, the Department for the Economy, the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, Keeping Northern Ireland Beautiful, Queen’s University Belfast, Ulster University, and practitioners and other experts in this field. The development of a Sustainability Hub, available on CCEA’s website, to provide information and support for teachers, stakeholders and young people. The development of four videos which can be used to encourage discussion and critical reflection on the nature and role of Education for Sustainable Development across the curriculum. Members of the expert group also analysed a sample of CCEA qualifications to identify to what extent sustainability matters are covered in the specifications and assess the scope for further embedding of climate change, green growth and sustainability concepts.More broadly, the findings of the independent Strategic Review of the Northern Ireland Curriculum have just been published.  The report identifies significant weaknesses in the current curriculum, which had not been formally reviewed in over 17 years.  It recommends that my Department develops a new curriculum framework for Northern Ireland which has greater specificity about the important knowledge, concepts and skills that children should learn at each stage of their education.  I have established a Curriculum Taskforce to lead on this work who will work collaboratively with educators across Northern Ireland to translate the review’s recommendations into a practical, ambitious curriculum framework.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, further to the judgement outlined in JR SCO12735, to outline whether he will ensure that a Rural Needs Impact Assessment will be carried out for any new policy or plan arising from the Education Authority review of  Published Admission Number allocations by the Pre-School Education Group.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has completed the review that I requested to be carried out of its processes for the allocation of funded pre-school education places to non-statutory pre-school education providers and submitted their draft report to me for consideration.  The EA has confirmed that any changes to the PAN allocation process arising from it will, as necessary, be subject to equality, rural and other relevant assessments.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail an update on the publication of the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy.
answer_text: I have committed to bringing forward a comprehensive Early Learning and Childcare Strategy for Executive consideration and public consultation in the autumn.  I made a Written Ministerial Statement on 15 May setting out my early learning and childcare measures for 2025/26, including next steps.  The Statement can be found at the link below.Written Ministerial Statement - Early Learning and Childcare Measures 2025/26 | Department of Education

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on investment in teacher professional learning, as set out in the Minister's oral statement on the Independent Review of Education.
answer_text: The TransformED NI Strategy and its accompanying Delivery Plan (published on 11 March and 29 April 2025 respectively) set out my key priorities for teacher professional learning, informed by the Independent Review of Education.  The Delivery Plan sets out the implementation approach, key delivery partners, indicative timescales and estimated investment for all actions relating to teacher professional learning. Key actions include an expanded programme of Teacher Induction; renewed and expanded provision for school leaders at all levels; additional funding for schools to purchase high-quality professional learning to meet their needs; improved access to education research for teachers through the Making Best Practice, Common Practice programme; an online science of learning programme for both primary and post primary schools; and professional learning communities to support subject-specific professional practice and collaborative professional learning clusters in partnership with the Higher Education Institutions. Implementation has commenced across a range of areas.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail an update on the creation of Local Impact Teams for the start of the 2025-26 academic year
answer_text: I shortly expect to receive a comprehensive update from the Education Authority on the creation of the Local IMPACT Teams service delivery model for 2025 along with what schools can expect for September 2025. Online school engagement sessions are also scheduled for delivery on Wednesday 11 June and officials from my department will be in attendance.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail an update on the strategic review of the classroom assistant employment model.
answer_text: The strategic review of the classroom assistant employment model is being taken forward by the Education Authority (EA).The EA has advised that the project infrastructure has been put in place, with an initial scoping exercise carried out and a range of workstreams developed. These workstreams cover the development of: (i) a new employment model with new job descriptions; (ii) a digital non-teaching staff bank to support central recruitment; (iii) a professional development pathway; and (iv) professional standards.Significant and continuous engagement with stakeholders has been undertaken over the past 12 months, and the EA plans to initiate a further engagement process at the beginning of the 2025/26 academic year. This will help to ensure that any new employment model meets the needs of all stakeholders, in particular children and young people.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) to provide an update on his Department's work to establish a regional policy and specification for the future supply of wigs being undertaken alongside the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust; and (ii) to detail the engagement undertaken with the Procurement and Logistics Service as part of this review.
To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the publication of: (i) the Special Education Needs (SEN) Graduated Response Framework; and (ii) the SEN Outcomes Framework.
answer_text: The Special Education Needs (SEN) Graduated Response Framework has now been published on the EA website and is available to the general public through the following link:https://send.eani.org.uk/grfI have recently approved the Special Educational Needs (SEN) Reform Agenda and Delivery Plan Outcomes Framework and expect this to be published before the end of June 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail an update on the publication of a Special Educational Needs Policy Statement.
answer_text: My officials are currently preparing a draft Special Educational Needs (SEN) Policy Statement which will come to me for consideration in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the appointment of a Commissioner for Public Appointments for Northern Ireland.

To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 26997/22-27, to outline whether there are any strands of work in the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy to deliver support for Children Looked After in pre-school settings.
answer_text: Any expansion to nursery and pre-school school settings of the Children Looked After Advisory Service provided through the Education Authority currently, will be subject to the findings of the evaluation of this service, which is planned for the 2025/26 academic year.Officials from across the relevant policy areas are liaising to ensure that the additional support needs of children looked after in pre-school setting are included within the comprehensive Early Learning and Childcare Strategy. While the Strategy will set out an ambitious plan for the longer-term.  It will be for the Executive to decide on the level of funding it is prepared to allocate to this.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 26996/22-27, when she anticipates advice from her officials will be provided on the key issues impacting the financial sustainability of childcare providers.
answer_text: The cross-Departmental Steering Group has received the draft Final Research Report on Targeted Business Support for Childcare Providers and is considering its findings. Advice from my officials will follow once this is complete.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education,pursuant to AQW 26830/22-27, whether he plans to undertake a review of the effectiveness of the Education Authority's Specialist Settings Support Team, as recommended in the November 2024 Education and Training Inspectorate report on Specialist Provision in Mainstream Schools.
answer_text: There are no immediate plans to undertake a review of the effectiveness of the Education Authority's Specialist Settings Support Team.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on work undertaken by the Education Authority's Maintenance Service on how it can provide the most efficient and effective model for schools to procure low-level maintenance works.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised that it is working to establish a process to provide autonomy to schools in the delivery of low value ‘tenant’ maintenance works. This will initially be limited to internal decoration and replacement of floor finishes. The EA is currently reviewing options for delivery which will provide delegated responsibilities and protect the interests of school leaders.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the total budget allocated to the Pathway Fund for 2025-26.
answer_text: The total 2025-26 Pathway Fund budget is £6.626m.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail what funding has been allocated to the Belfast Wide Early Years pilot project for this financial year.
answer_text: Funding of £115k was allocated to the Belfast Wide Early Years (BWEY) pilot project for the period April to June 2025 which will ensure continuation of provision until the end of this academic year.Any further funding decisions in relation to the BWEY pilot will be taken in consideration of the independent evaluation of it and of the actions detailed in the Special Educational Needs (SEN) Reform Agenda and associated Delivery Plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the Education Authority review of how it delivers maintenance and minor works to schools.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised that its Chief Executive has engaged the services of an external reviewer to act as a ‘critical friend’ and undertake an independent assessment of EA’s capital works processes.  This external report is in development. It is currently anticipated that the detail of the report will be presented to the EA Board at its meeting in June 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail: (i) how many applications were made for the Pathway Fund 2025/26; (ii) how many applications were awarded funding.
answer_text: A total of 933 applications were made to the 2025-26 Pathway Fund.  31 of the applications were ineligible for an award.  All 902 eligible applicants were funded and have received their 2025-26 award - an increase from 175 awards to all eligible (Stream A and B) applicants in 2024-25.  A breakdown of the applications is provided in the table below:Funding StreamApplications receivedApplications fundedStream A (up to £40,000)161149Stream B (up to £20,000)8268Stream C (up to £500)Introduced 2025-26690685Total933902

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 27098/22-27, if he will publish costings for the actions contained within the Special Educational Needs Reform Delivery Plan.
answer_text: The high level, estimated cost of full implementation of the Special Educational Needs (SEN) Reform Delivery Plan actions is £570m over 5 years, as stated in the published document. Detailed costings for delivery of each action continue to be finessed through the development of implementation plans and through the business case process, in collaboration with key delivery partners, prioritising the actions to be delivered with the Public Sector Transformation funding secured.  This working detail is not planned for publication; however, there will be full transparency and accountability on expenditure.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 26753-22-27, to detail: (i) how many specialist settings the planned 63 staff within the Education Authority's Specialist Settings Support Team currently support; (ii) how many they will be required support from September 2025.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised that:The EA's Specialist Settings Support Team (SSST) currently support 343 Specialist Provision Classes which have been set up since 2021.The EA’s SSST will be required to support 710 Specialist Provision Classes (585 legacy classes and approximately 125 new classes) with the planned 63 staff currently being recruited.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any support the Education Authority's Specialist Setting Support Team provides to specialist settings in schools.
answer_text: The EA’s Specialist Setting Support Team (SSST), is a multi-service team, providing integrated support for Specialist Provision Classes in Mainstream Schools. Working with existing services, and drawing on best practice from established settings, SSST works alongside staff in specialist provision classes, supporting delivery of high-quality provision and educational experiences for children and young people. SSST staff work across multiple schools to provide professional learning opportunities, advice and on the ground support to leaders, teachers, and classroom assistants. The model is designed to complement current service delivery models and ensure a connected approach across EA services to streamline support for settings.Support is provided before set-up of a new specialist provision and afterwards and includes universal and targeted support for both school staff and parents as well as specialist support for children and young people.  The EA delivers support both online and in person, for senior leadership, teachers, classroom assistants and parents. It has a range of programmes across all phases (Early Years, Primary and Post Primary), designed to support settings with curriculum and assessment, quality of provision and staff development and leadership.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education if there are plans to remove the Education Authority (EA)'s Essential Car User Allowance payment for staff from the EA Special Education Needs Transition Service.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has informed me that it is currently reviewing its Travel and Subsistence Scheme in order to ensure a fair and appropriate reimbursement policy is in place for all staff. A 12 week engagement period with Trade Unions is ongoing, after which a revised scheme will be implemented for all staff. This engagement includes discussion on both the eligibility criteria for essential car user allowance and the mileage rates that apply. It is not possible at this stage to confirm the impact of the revised scheme on any group of staff as the discussions with trade unions are not yet complete and the detail of the revised scheme is not yet known. EA is committed to keeping staff up to date as these discussions develop.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on what he is doing to provide more flexibility for schools to make purchases of equipment and materials when they can secure better value than Education Authority approved suppliers.
answer_text: As the managing authority for controlled schools, the funding authority for all grant-aided schools and the Centre of Procurement Excellence (CoPE) for education, the Education Authority (EA) is responsible for ensuring that effective procurement practice is embedded across the education sector.All schools are required to operate within the procurement legal framework and adhere to Northern Ireland Public Procurement Policy and all relevant Northern Ireland Procurement Policy Notes (PPNs).  Notwithstanding, I share the concerns of our school stakeholders in relation to procurement processes and the benefit of offering our schools greater flexibility and reduced bureaucracy in this area.It is important to note that the EA, as education’s CoPE, conducts thorough consultations with schools before establishing contracts.  In addition, schools can procure goods and services directly from suppliers for lower-value purchases, in accordance with PPN 04/21.The recent revision of PPN 04/21 Procurement Control Limits in March 2025 increased the thresholds which determine the procurement processes for lower value purchases which all contracting authorities, including schools, must follow. The threshold at which a contracting authority must evidence two price checks has increased from £5,000 to £10,000.  Given this update to PPN 04/21, I have recently instructed the EA to increase the schools’ procurement control limit to £10,000 for purchases which require two evidenced price checks.  This change will provide schools with more flexibility for those procurements below £10,000 and which are not already available through an existing framework.  This change has been communicated to schools via numerous EA channels including C2K and the EA Procurement portal.In addition to this change, I have instructed my officials to engage with the EA to explore further options available specifically in relation to schools’ use of frameworks.  The EA is exploring options for providing schools with greater flexibility in this area of spend, including an assessment of the potential benefits, risks and impact of any changes which may be considered or piloted.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he will increase the priority level applied to the minor works project to deliver a multi-purpose hall for St Francis' Primary, Drumaroad, to reflect that the modular unit housing the dining facility may need replaced.
answer_text: The minor works scheme at St Francis’ Primary School is currently considered a Priority Three scheme i.e. curriculum based minor works scheme. Given the design work, vesting of land and planning approval required for this project, it is being progressed to pre-tender stage. These stages would be required regardless of the prioritisation of the scheme. A decision to release to tender will be taken based on the prioritisation and the budget available at that time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities if his Department has assessed the effectiveness of a ban on no fault evictions in other jurisdictions.
answer_text: My Department has made no assessment on the effectiveness of a ban on no-fault evictions in other jurisdictions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to outline what support is available to renters who face a no fault eviction.
answer_text: Those receiving a Notice to Quit from private rented sector accommodation may avail of homelessness assistance under the Housing (NI) Order 1988.   This includes households considered to be threatened with homelessness within 28 days. As set out in the Order, a household may be owed the full housing duty and can avail of support including temporary accommodation and storage of belongings where appropriate. All households approaching the Housing Executive for support, regardless of whether they are owed the full housing duty, are entitled to advice and assistance, including information relating to housing options and signposting to other relevant support.  My Department also funds Housing Rights which provides a wide range of services including helplines.  It also provides a meditation service to help landlords and tenants resolve issues and sustain tenancies.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any plans he has to introduce a ban on no fault evictions.
answer_text: I am already taking forward work to introduce much longer notice to quit periods, as mandated by the Assembly in the Private Tenancies Act (NI), 2022. This change will greatly improve security of tenure for individuals and families in the Private Rent Sector. I therefore have no plans to introduce a ban on no fault evictions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 25078/22-27, when the phased, costed operational plan which will sit behind the Special Educational Needs Reform Delivery Plan will be published.
answer_text: The operational plan for the Special Educational Needs (SEN) Reform Delivery Plan is a working document to support planning and as such it is not intended that it will be published.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 15912/22-27 and AQO 1108/22-27, for an update on any plans he has to expand interventions as part of the Early Intervention Transformation Programme to pre-school and nursery school settings which enroll Children Looked After.
answer_text: As previously advised, the focus on early intervention in respect of Children Looked After (CLA), has been to make provisions at a primary level and this has been delivered by the Education Authority under the auspices of the ‘Primary Children Looked After Advisory Service’. An independent evaluation of this service is planned for during Academic year 2025/26 and officials will then bring forward recommendations for my consideration. It is important to note that any expansion into early years settings will be contingent upon the availability of financial resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he will establish a steering group to pilot and act on examples of provision which can meet the needs of pupils who present with significantly challenging behaviour, as recommended in the Education and Training Inspectorate report 'An Evaluation of the Provision to meet the Needs of Pupils with Persistent and Challenging Behaviour in Special Schools 2012-13'.
answer_text: I would refer the Member to the answer to question AQW 25379/22-27.The answer is contained in the Written Answers Booklet for week ending 16 May 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail how his Department has delivered on the recommendations of the Education and Training Inspectorate report 'An Evaluation of the Provision to meet the Needs of Pupils with Persistent and Challenging Behaviour in Special Schools 2012-13'.
answer_text: I would refer the member to the answer provided for AQW 25379/22-27. Published in the weekly answers booklet week ending 18 April 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 26142/22-27, to outline what recommendations in the report from the Review on the Use of Temporary and Substitute Teachers (i) have been actioned; and (ii) require additional resources.
answer_text: A detailed assessment of the recommendations from the Review on the Use of Temporary and Substitute Teachers has been jointly carried out with the trade unions.Recommendations which (i) have been actioned; and (ii) require additional resources has been deposited in the Assembly Library. It has also been agreed that the Independent Review of Teachers’ Workload, including the Heads of Agreement which was part of the 2024/25 Teachers’ pay agreement, will form the basis on which teachers’ and school leaders’ workload issues will be addressed going forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the work that has been completed to date by her Department on the development of a targeted business support scheme for the childcare sector.
answer_text: My Department is leading on cross-Departmental work to consider research on the key issues impacting the financial sustainability of childcare providers. Once I have received advice on these issues I will move quickly to provide my advice to the Minister of Education.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline how many Special Needs Education transition plans that have been created within the last 5 years have agreed a pathway for transfer to adult health care.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised that:This information has not been held centrally until now. From September 2025 we will be holding this via EA Connect.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when he will confirm if any additional resources will be given to the Pathway Fund.
answer_text: I have increased the maximum level of award for the Pathway Fund from £30,000 to £40,000 for Stream A and from £15,000 to £20,000 for Stream B, in light of increased costs of delivering services.  I also plan to introduce an additional Stream C to provide specific support to Childminders and Approved Home Childcarers (AHCs).However, the DE budget for Pathway cannot meet the additional demand and my plans for expansion. As you are aware, I have sought Executive agreement to provide additional funding to eligible Pathway applicants from the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy (ELCS) ring-fenced allocation in 2025-26.  If Executive approval is received, DE will confirm the additional funding with Early Years (EYO) which administers the Pathway Fund on behalf of the Department, who will notify recipient settings.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline: (i) how families who are supported by the Education Authority's Special Education Needs Transition Service find out about this service; and (ii) when these families find out about access to the service and the support it provides.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised that communications identifying the Transition Service and the relevant pathways to access the Transition Service issue to families/relevant service users at the start of the transition process in year 10.  Additional opportunities for awareness and knowledge of the service may be obtained by family attendance at school-based transitions events or during Annual Reviews.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline how his Department will ensure that all young people with a statement of special educational need, have access to independent travel training programmes from the earliest stage.
answer_text: The Post 16 curriculum in Special Schools will generally include aspects related to the development of life skills, such as independence and social skills, online safety and travel training. Mainstream schools may do this to a lesser extent, but it will depend on the profile of learners within their school.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) if he plans to reinstate funding for The Blues Programme which was previously run by Action for Children in post-primary schools; and (ii) for his assessment of the impact that the programme had.
answer_text: My department has no record of directly funding ‘The Blues Programme’ delivered by Action for Children.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he plans to undertake a review of the effectiveness of the Education Authority's Specialist Settings Support Team, as recommended in the Education and Training Inspectorate's report on Specialist Provision in Mainstream Schools.
answer_text: The Education Authority has advised that: An early evaluation of the Education Authority’s (EA) Specialist Setting Support Team (SSST) was undertaken by the Education and Training Inspectorate (ETI) in 2019. Since then, in response to a significantly increased number of settings and the associated need, the service has expanded and developed, particularly in the past two years.The EA SSST complete regular evaluations of the support provided to specialist educational provisions in mainstream schools including evaluative discussions with setting staff, review of individual learner plans, parent evaluations, setting case studies, and baseline self-evaluation frameworks.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any systems in place to monitor, measure, control and report on the activities of the Transition Service for young people aged 14+ who have a Statement of Special Educational Needs.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised that:The EA Transitions Service use Capita One (Management Information System) and EA Connect (Annual Review portal) for recording of activities regarding young people aged 14+ who have a Statement of Educational Needs in Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any engagement undertaken with children and young people on their experiences of the Transition Service for young people aged 14+ who have a Statement of Special Educational Needs.
answer_text: As part of the End to End (E2E) Review of Special Educational Needs (SEN), a series of engagement events took place with young people who were going through (or had gone through) the transitions process and parents/carers.  The Education Authority’s (EA) Transitions Service engages with young people during annual reviews of statements, school leaver events, careers appointments and transitions visits.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust respite stays at Greenhill that have been cancelled in the last 12 months, including the reason for cancellation.
answer_text: South Eastern Trust can advise that in the past 12 months there have been 29 overnights cancelled which has impacted upon 10 young people.   Cancellations are mostly attributed to absences of staff or the Trust having to reprioritise the allocation of an overnight Short Break in the case of an emergency.  3 overnights were cancelled at the request of two families.  The Trust endeavours to deliver community based Short Breaks or day care child minding to those young people who have had an overnight Short Break cancelled. Those community Short Breaks are delivered through the Community Outreach Service , LynC Outreach, the Social Work Assistants attached to the Social Work teams or a combination of Direct Payments.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the current number of vacancies at the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust Greenhill respite facility.
answer_text: The Trust can advise there are currently 9 vacancies in Greenhill.  The Trust is currently running a bespoke advertisement and recruitment campaign and interviews took place on the 17 May 2025 and outcomes are still being processed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the timescale of the June Monitoring Round.

To ask the Minister of Education if his Department will develop an accessible, quick-read child-friendly guide detailing the various types of specialist provisions and classes, as recommended within the Education and Training Inspectorate's report on Specialist Provision in Mainstream Schools.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised that:As part of their ongoing commitment to enhance resources available to families, schools and settings, the Education Authority is developing an accessible, quick-read child-friendly guide detailing the various types of specialist provisions and classes, as recommended within the Education and Training Inspectorate's report on Specialist Provision in Mainstream Schools.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether enhancing the Pathway Fund from the £50 million budget allocated for the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy in 2025-26 requires approval from his Department.
answer_text: The stabilisation and expansion of the Pathway Fund requires expenditure approval from my Department. A business case supporting this request was submitted to the Department of Finance on 7 May 2025. This was part of a wider business case seeking expenditure approval for the full package of Early Learning and Childcare interim measures in 2025-26.The business case is currently being reviewed by my officials as a matter of priority.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on (i) the installation of a pedestrian crossing point on Killinchy Road, Comber, and (ii) the resurfacing of the Killinchy Road.
answer_text: Road safety remains a high priority for my Department and we are committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and address the needs of all road users.(i)  I can advise that an uncontrolled crossing with a pedestrian refuge island will be provided as part of planned resurfacing works. This will be located within the hatched area near the Texaco garage to aid pedestrians in crossing the A22 Killinchy Road safely.(ii) I am pleased to announce that resurfacing of the A22 Killinchy Road, Comber from the A21 Newtownards Road to Drumhirk Road is programmed to commence 2nd June 2025.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the appointment of a Commissioner for Public Appointments.
answer_text: We are pleased to report that the competition to appoint a Commissioner for Public Appointments for NI (CPANI) launched on 10 March. A Commissioner will be appointed as soon as possible, following the conclusion of the appointment process. Officials will conduct an evaluation of the process once complete.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline (i) how many staff make up the Education Authority's Specialist Settings Support Team; and (ii) his assessment of the current level of resourcing for this team in relation to the demand for this service.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised that:A core team of specialist staff is in place and recruitment is ongoing to add an additional 15 staff to enhance and stabilise current support, bringing the total to 63 full time equivalent staff. This will address the growing needs and ensure that all existing specialist classes are adequately supported by September 2025.The service is ensuring alignment with the recommendations laid out in the recently published SEN Reform Agenda and will focus on ensuring that all specialist provisions in Northern Ireland receive the necessary support to thrive. To achieve this, the recruitment process noted above is ongoing.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to outline what work has been done to cost the provision of a facial palsy in-reach service.
answer_text: On average 3-5 patients are assessed as suitable for Facial Palsy surgery per annum in Northern Ireland.  These referrals are managed through the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust as part of a Network arrangement with a specialist provider in Great Britain.  This activity represents a very small component of the Facial Palsy care pathway as the majority of patients are managed locally across all Trusts and supported by colleagues from Primary care. In-reach services are provided as part of the total commissioning arrangement for Plastic Surgery Services in South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust.   Most of the facial palsy assessment and treatment is provided locally, through the local hospital specialties.  For those patients requiring specialist surgical/botox treatment, SPPG send most of these patients to the UK via an Extra Contractual (ECR) Arrangement.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education why the Education Authority does not collect data on the number of pupils with Social Behaviour and Emotional Wellbeing (SBEW) needs in specialist classes, apart from those who have SBEW as their primary need.
answer_text: The Recording Special Educational Needs (SEN) and Medical Categories guidance document issued by the department, under Circular 2019/13, sets out the categories under which pupils with SEN and medical conditions, can be recorded on a school’s SEN and/or Medical Register. Many pupils with SEN have more than one type of SEN or difficulty.  For pupils with a statement of SEN who are placed in specialist settings, additional needs including Social Behaviour and Emotional Wellbeing, will therefore be recorded, if identified, and reported in the annual school census data.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to outline why a facial palsy in-reach service has not been developed.
To ask the Minister of Education when he will answer AQW 26269/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 26269/22-27 was answered on 19 May 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to outline (i) how much funding was allocated to the Plastics Trauma Clinic in the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust to develop in-reach services; and (ii) how this funding was spent, in each year since 2019.
answer_text: My Department is not aware of any in-reach funding for the Plastics Trauma Clinic allocated to the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust and is not therefore in a position to provide the profile of information.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline how he will ensure that the designation of a school as Specialist Provision in Mainstream Schools appropriately reflects the needs of pupils.
answer_text: The Education Authority has advised that:The Framework for Specialist Provision in Mainstream Schools sets out criteria and indicators to identify mainstream schools in which to establish additional specialist provision classes to help meet the needs of children and young people with special educational needs (SEN) within their local community.Every child in a specialist provision class has a Statement of Special Educational Needs, and the monitoring and evaluation of outcomes for these children is achieved primarily through the review process of their Statement, and the tracking of progress against the targets on their Individual Education Plan (IEP) or expected outcomes on their Personal Learning Plan (PLP). Where a change of place is recommended as part of the annual process the Education Authority will review the placement in line with the individual needs of pupils to enable them to reach their full potential.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 21627/22-27.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she will provide the necessary funding to NI Water to bring unadopted sewerage and storm drainage systems in private developments up to standard, when it has not been possible to either make a developer liable or the bond has been insufficient to fund the required works.
answer_text: The primary issue with unadopted developments, is that they remain private developments. In some cases, the houses were sold to the residents without the sewerage infrastructure being adopted or having an agreement in place for future adoption by NI Water.In other cases, an agreement and bond may be in place, but the value of the bond is insufficient to cover the cost of the works required to make good the site.In both cases, neither the Department nor NI Water has any legal remit to carry out works in private developments, with the responsibility for the development resting with the developer or the residents.  In such circumstances, NI Water makes every effort to encourage developers to complete their sites to an adoptable standard so that they may be adopted.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she will fund NI Water to deliver the necessary improvements to sewerage and storm drainage systems in the Magheraknock Park development, Ballynahinch.
answer_text: Magheraknock Park, Ballynahinch is a private development and neither the Department nor NI Water has any legal remit to carry out works in private developments.  The responsibility to construct the sewerage infrastructure to a standard which NI Water will adopt, rests with the developer or the residents.NI Water makes every effort to encourage developers to complete their sites to an appropriate standard so that they may be adopted.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she will provide NI Water with funding to facilitate the periodic inspection of sewerage and storm drainage systems, subject to Article 161 approval, whilst under construction.
answer_text: Developers are responsible for how they undertake their construction, and where they intend to have sites adopted, they are entirely responsible to ensure that they construct the associated drainage infrastructure to an adoptable standard.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 25329/22-27, how these outcomes will be measured.
answer_text: The Outcomes Framework for the Special Educational Needs (SEN) Reform Agenda and Delivery Plan is in the final stages of development and is expected to be completed and published in the coming weeks.Using an outcomes-based accountability approach, performance measures for individual actions, including the development of a Transitional Support Programme, will be developed as part of the implementation process in collaboration with the relevant stakeholders and will be monitored via Report Cards.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 21714/22-27.
To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 25625/22-27, to detail (i) the scope of the Pupil Allocation Number allocation processes review; and (ii) when this review will be completed.
answer_text: While the operational processes regarding the management of the pre-school admission process and the allocation of funded pre-school education places to non-statutory settings are entirely within the remit of the Education Authority (EA), I have asked the EA to carry out a review of the current systems and processes and propose potential options for change and improvement, addressing the need for a fair balance between funded provision in statutory and non-statutory pre-school settings and greater clarity and certainty for non-statutory pre-school providers in terms of their Pupil Allocation Number (PAN) allocations over the short, medium and long term. To ensure that any relevant recommendations can be incorporated within the draft Early Learning and Childcare Strategy which I intend to bring to the Executive later this year, I have requested a copy of the EA review proposals by end May 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when an Outcomes Framework for the Special Educational Needs Reform Agenda Delivery Plan will be published.
answer_text: The Outcomes Framework for the Special Educational Needs (SEN) Reform Agenda and Delivery Plan is in the final stages of development and is expected to be completed and published in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how his Department is collecting feedback from pupils, parents and staff in relation to their experience of newly established Specialist Provision in Mainstream Schools providers.
answer_text: The Education and Training Inspectorate published part one of an evaluation on Specialist Provisions in Mainstream Schools (November 2024).  To inform the report, inspectors held discussions with key members of staff, including principals, special educational needs co-ordinators and other school co-ordinators, teachers and classroom assistants from the specialist provisions.  Inspectors also met with a number of learners and with 20 parents remotely.The second part of this evaluation, which commenced in September 2024 has continued to take account of the views and lived experiences of learners, parents and staff.  Inspectors met with the leaders, teachers and classroom assistants working in the 134 Specialist Provision in Mainstream classes visited as part of the evaluation.  Inspectors held meetings with 100 learners (35 in primary schools and 65 in post-primary schools) to hear their views and also met with 100 parents who have children attending Specialist Provisions in Mainstream Schools (45 in primary schools and 55 in post-primary schools).  The Education Authority has advised that the Specialist Setting Support Team completes regular evaluations to collect feedback in relation to specialist educational provisions in mainstream schools including evaluative discussions with setting staff, review of individual learner plans, parent evaluations, setting case studies, and baseline self-evaluation frameworks.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether the Priorities for Youth policy review, due to be launched in early 2026, will confirm that community and voluntary sector will be the primary delivery mechanism for youth services.
answer_text: The Education Authority has statutory responsibility for the provision for youth services as set out in The Education and Libraries (Northern Ireland) Order 1986.The Priorities for Youth policy review commenced in September 2023.  My officials are gathering, reviewing and assessing evidence from a broad range of sources including a Young Person’s Reference Group; a Policy Reference Group; a Cross-Departmental Group; five thematic Sub-Groups; the Project Board; the Education and Training Inspectorate; relevant research reports and other policy areas within my department.  The review findings and evidence based policy options will be brought to me for consideration during summer 2025.  This will be followed by a public consultation process.  I cannot pre-determine the outcome of the review including any proposed changes to the current legislative position whereby the Education Authority has a statutory duty to deliver youth services.Quarterly updates on the policy review are available on the Department’s  website at https://www.education-ni.gov.uk/articles/priorities-youth-pfy-policy-review. An update for April 2025 has recently been added.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any enabling actions included in the Special Educational Needs Reform Agenda Delivery Plan that have been commenced.
answer_text: Work has commenced on all enabling Year 1 and Year 2 actions, with some actions at a more advanced stage than others.  Priority is being given to operationalisation of the projects being funded under the Public Sector Transformation Fund.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any Year 2 actions included in the Special Educational Needs Reform Agenda Delivery Plan that have been commenced.
answer_text: Work has commenced on all enabling Year 1 and Year 2 actions, with some actions at a more advanced stage than others.  Priority is being given to operationalisation of the projects being funded under the Public Sector Transformation Fund.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any Year 1 actions included in the Special Educational Needs Reform Agenda Delivery Plan that have been commenced.
answer_text: Work has commenced on all enabling Year 1 and Year 2 actions, with some actions at a more advanced stage than others.  Priority is being given to operationalisation of the projects being funded under the Public Sector Transformation Fund.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 25871/22-27, to detail (i) the amount spent on funding pre-school places for penultimate age children; (ii) any consideration given to using this funding on a universal programme for two to three year olds; and (iii) any legislation that requires a penultimate age child to be given a pre-school placement if requested.
answer_text: I would refer you to my previous reply to AQW 23664/22-27 which provides the information requested in the first part of your question. The answer is contained in the weekly answer booklet w/e 21 March 2025. The Pre-school funding for penultimate-age children cannot currently be used for other purposes due to the statutory duty set out in Part V of the Education (Northern Ireland) Order 1998.However, provision for 2-year-olds is being considered as part of the development of an Executive Early Learning and Childcare (ELC) Strategy which I will bring to the Executive in the autumn.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he intends to allocate capital funding to the Education Authority Library Service as part of the 2025-26 budget allocation.
answer_text: I do not intend to allocate capital funding to the Education Authority Library Service this financial year as they have not indicated any planned capital expenditure for this area in 2025/26.The Education Authority is progressing procurement for a schools’ library solution.  However, it is expected to be a recurrent cost rather than capital.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when the procurement process for the provision of wigs for patients will be concluded.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail the outcome of any discussions with the Regional Procurement Board on the provision of wigs for patients.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail how he is ensuring that all Health and Social Care Trusts are providing the best service to patients requiring wigs.
To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of the current Allied Health Professional to pupil ratios.
answer_text: It is acknowledged that pressures across the AHP workforce mean there are significant challenges in meeting the growing demand for services, including meeting the needs of children with SEN.  The current AHP to pupil rations are not adequate.The Public Health Agency, in partnership with Education sector colleagues, is leading a comprehensive analysis of the therapeutic needs of children with SEN in Special Schools. Three SEN project leads have been recruited to date specifically to consider the data to inform the needs of children with SEN. The fourth SEN Project Lead is due to take up post in June 2025 and the remaining Trust is actively progressing with recruitment. This work is planned to be completed on a phased basis by March 2026. At the same time, I am mindful that the Programme for Government highlights access to therapeutic support for children with SEN more broadly. To ensure we make the greatest impact, the Chief AHP Officer has convened a cross-system meeting in June to get a clearer picture of the current gap, how the different pieces of work connect and what our collective plan will be moving forward. Cross Departmental working also remains critical to the delivery of a collaborative approach which fully supports children and young people with SEN. I and my officials continue to work closely with the Department of Education on a whole system approach to SEN reform.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the Review of Suspensions and Expulsions.
answer_text: I will be considering the draft report on the review of suspension and expulsion arrangements shortly. Once I have considered the draft report, I will determine the next steps to be taken.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 13934/22-27, for an update on his Department’s engagement with children and young people in relation to improved attendance.
answer_text: The “Miss School. Miss Out” strategy remains under review. Therefore, as yet there has not been formal engagement with children and young people. The topic of attendance has however been a key theme in the ongoing RAISE Programme engagement with children and young people.  Officials have also attended various events throughout the year and have been taking on board the opinions and concerns of both pupils and parents in relation to attendance at school.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether the new Education Information Solutions system will record the number of children not attending school due to emotionally-based school avoidance.
answer_text: My officials requested the Education Information Solutions (EdIS) team to initiate the development of a new absence code for Emotionally Based School Non Attendance. This code will be made available in the current School Information Management Systems (SIMS) and the new Schools Management System (Bromcom) over the Summer 2025 break and should be in operation for the start of the new academic year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any role the Education Training Inspectorate has in monitoring the appropriateness of schools' admissions criteria.
answer_text: The Education and Training Inspectorate (ETI) does not have a specific role to monitor the appropriateness of schools' admissions criteria.  During individual organisation inspection, ETI evaluates the quality of the strategic and corporate governance of a school in relation to how effectively it supports the provision of high-quality education for the children or young people.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether enhancing the Pathway Fund from the £50 million budget allocated for the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy in 2025-26 requires Executive approval.
answer_text: Release of the £50m for Early Learning and Childcare (ELC) to my Department was contingent upon the Executive approving my ELC Strategy plan for 2025-26. As I outlined in COR 0507-2025, I sought Executive approval for that plan in a paper circulated to Ministerial colleagues on the 8 April.  It included a proposal to enhance the Pathway Fund budget.  The Executive approved the plan on 15 May 2025.This additional funding means that all Pathway applicants which have met the eligibility requirements in 2025-26 can receive an award.  Early Years settings eligible for Pathway funding, will soon receive confirmation of their 2025-26 award from Early Years (EYO) which administers the Pathway Fund on behalf of my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the rights that parents hold to challenge a school's published admissions criteria.
answer_text: The setting of admissions criteria is solely a matter for the Boards of Governors (BoGs) of individual schools and my department has no power to set criteria.  My department does, however, issue guidance on the transfer process, including on recommended admissions criteria, which BoGs must by law “have regard to”.  There is no route to challenge a school’s published admissions criteria, other than via Judicial Review.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when the Education Authority review into the price of school meals will be completed.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) is continuing to progress its review of its price of school meals across all school sectors, taking into account both the increases in costs that have been experienced in recent times across the catering sector and the potential impact of any rise in price on families who continue to experience a rise in their cost of living. The EA will conclude this review as soon as possible and any resultant change in prices will be communicated to all relevant stakeholders.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether his Department has the power to direct a school that uses admissions criteria not in keeping with DE Circular 2022/15.
