answer_text: The following table outlines the proposed investment in the Curriculum-Led Capital Investment Programme in the 2024-25 financial year:Programme AreaFunding in 2024-25Computer Equipment£5.4mOutdoor learning project £4mProvision of defibrillators in all schoolsc£735kProvision of mobile phone inhibiting devicesc£250kCall for pilot of covered outdoor play spacesLimited spend (planning)Call for synthetic pitchesLimited spend (planning)Call for post-primary sports hallsLimited spend (planning)Total Spend in 2024-25£10.385m

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 18494/22-27, to detail (i) the work that was included in the Strategic Partner element of the Education Authority's contract with Fujitsu; and (ii) whether the new school management system is still being delivered by Fujitsu.
answer_text: The Strategic Partner element of the service was the provision of improved and transformed services to Schools, the Authority and Education Stakeholders that provides End Users within the scope of functionality of the current service with enhanced capabilities and improved learning experiences, all supported by a secure and scalable modern architecture and technical infrastructure that provides an end to end fully integrated, interoperable digital education service for Schools.The new school management system will not be delivered by Fujitsu.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, further to the review of the eligibility criteria for free school meals and uniform grants, whether he will also review the current quantum of uniform grant payments.
answer_text: The school uniform grant is available to offer financial support to low-income families as a contribution towards the costs of school uniforms. The EA raised its school uniform grant rates by 20% in 2022/23 following an additional allocation of funding by the previous Minister of Education and this has been maintained at this level.Severe budgetary pressures mean that it has not been possible to allocate any additional funding to the EA to implement a further rise in the rates of uniform grant for the 2024/25 academic year. I will keep this under consideration in terms of the budget for 2025/26.While I would wish to be in a position to allocate additional funding to the EA to enable a rise in the uniform grant, this can only be done when it is affordable to do so.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, further to the October Monitoring Round, to detail any remaining financial pressures related to homelessness as set out in Departmental Management Board Minutes of 4 September 2024.
answer_text: DoF provided an additional Non-Ring-Fenced Resource allocation of £11.3m as part of October Monitoring. £6.7m was allocated to prevent the risk of homelessness service closures, to ensure that statutory obligations can continue to be met and to meet inescapable salary and administrative costs related to NIHE service delivery.DoF has commissioned the January Monitoring Round exercise with final returns due on the 3 January 2025 and my Department is currently working through the process to determine the most up to date financial position.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education why he did not include reinstating holiday hunger payments as an option for consideration in the Department's consultation on the review of the eligibility criteria for free school meals and uniform grants.
answer_text: Free school meals are only available during term time and the school holiday food grant was an entirely separate scheme introduced to support those children in most need outside of school term time. 	The scheme used free school meal entitlement to determine who should receive a grant payment. The school holiday food grant scheme was operated by my Department, on behalf of the Executive, up to March 2023. 	The reinstatement of such a scheme would not, in itself, result in any changes to the eligibility criteria for free school meals and uniform grants. 	It was therefore not appropriate for this to be included in a consultation on the options considering the future eligibility criteria for free school meals and uniform grants.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for his assessment of the Ards and North Down Borough Council plan to construct a footbridge over the Newtownards to Bangor dual carriageway, to facilitate access to Whitespots Country Park and the planned greenway.
To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 17770/22-27, to detail the metrics being used to assess the impact of the initiatives on teacher workload and overall working conditions.
answer_text: The Teachers’ Pay and Workload Agreement, TNC 2020/1 committed the Department, Employing Authorities and other arm’s length bodies to the introduction of a formal requirement on the Department, Employing Authorities and other arm’s length bodies to consider and consult on new initiatives and proposals which have the potential to increase teacher workload. Guidance for policy makers has been jointly devised with teaching trade unions on the development of new initiatives, including programmes and policies, advising that they should be assessed to identify the workload impact for teachers and school leaders prior to implementation.Part of this consultation involves ensuring that the workload impacts are fully considered in conjunction with teacher representatives to help inform the delivery of the policy and allow for any issues to be addressed at an early stage in the development process.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 17770/22-27, to provide specific timelines for the completion of the in progress recommendations identified in the joint framework with the Northern Ireland Teachers' Council.
answer_text: Through negotiation with the Northern Ireland Teachers’ Council (NITC) a framework has been agreed grouping the recommendations from the workforce reviews into themed areas which has formed a programme of work. The first phase of this work programme includes work on recommendations which is already in progress with completion dates from December 2024 until April 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he plans to make regulations as enabled by Article 23 of the Education and Libraries (Northern Ireland) Order 2003 to provide for the removal of members of a Board of Governors in specified circumstances.
answer_text: The Department has the power, under the Education and Libraries (Northern Ireland) Order 2003 (the 2003 Order), to make Regulations that provide for the removal and replacement of a Board of Governors (BoG) that is failing to provide effective leadership.I have asked officials to bring forward proposals for my consideration.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any education on homelessness that is included in the Northern Ireland curriculum.
answer_text: While homelessness is not explicitly outlined in the Northern Ireland curriculum, it can be addressed through various subjects and areas of learning.For example, through the Human Rights and Social Responsibility, Equality and Social Justice strands of Learning for Life and Work, students investigate how and why some people may experience inequality and social exclusion on the basis of their material circumstances in both local and global contexts.  This provides opportunities to discuss homelessness as a societal issue. The Council for the Curriculum, Examinations and Assessment have also produced useful online resources covering the topic of homelessness for Key Stages 1- 4.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) the reasons why the Education Authority ended its contract with Fujitsu for the development of a new school management system; and (ii) the anticipated impact this decision will have on the implementation and progress of the new school management system.
answer_text: The Education Authority and Fujitsu mutually agreed not to continue with the Strategic Partner element of the Strategic Partner / Schools Management System Contract. The Authority will continue the development of the new School Management System with implementation to begin this academic year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any investment being made in specialist learning disability social workers.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland Social Care Council (NISCC) is responsible for the regulation of Social Work education and training in Northern Ireland. NISCC are responsible for ensuring that there is sufficient, high quality qualifying and post-qualifying training in place to meet the range of needs of the Social Work workforce.The Social Work degree in Northern Ireland equips the workforce to work in a range of settings, including Learning Disability Services. On completion of the degree, all Social Workers in Northern Ireland are required to complete an Assessed Year in Employment.  They are then expected to further complete a Consolidation Award in Social Work, this means that those who work in Learning Disability Services will have extended knowledge and skills with regards to supporting people with Learning Disability and their families.Some Social Workers choose to progress into specialist practice areas within their programmes of care such as Adult Safeguarding or Palliative Care Social Work. Currently there is no specialist Learning Disability Social Work qualification. In addition to regional Social Work training courses, Social Workers within Learning Disability Services have access to a range of in-house training within their own Trusts. This training enables them to robustly support people with Learning Disabilities and their families.My Department has no current plans to make further additional investment in specialist Learning Disability Social Workers. The current training available to Social Workers is sufficient to equip them with the knowledge and skills to meet the needs of the Learning Disability population.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 16672/22-27, whether he will increase the level of funding available to the Fair Play Grant scheme, given that 65 settings out of 189 applicants received no funding to support the children in their care with additional needs.
answer_text: The Department of Health, through the Childcare Partnership, facilitates an annual grant scheme to ensure children with additional needs are included in early years services, known as Fair Play. The funding provides support measures with a focus on the individual needs of children who are accessing quality play and learning experiences in registered community, voluntary and private provision throughout the academic year. These support measures can and do include additional staffing capacity.The Childcare Partnership also provides free training for early years practitioners in a range of subjects to do with additional needs in tandem with this financial support. In the 2024/25 tranche 154 registered early years settings applied. The most recent figures indicate the number of applicant groups that benefitted from an award was 89 which included 343 children in their care. The funding for Fair Play 24/25 was enhanced by a further £250k through the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy bringing the total value of the fund to £675k April 2024-March 2025.I would love to increase funding, but the budget, which you supported, does not allow.The Childcare Partnership encourages engagement with the early years sector to maximise the reach of the fund.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his response to the recommendations in the joint Angel Eyes and Féach report entitled Education Access in Ireland for Students with Visual Impairment.
answer_text: The ‘Education Access in Ireland for Students with Vision Impairment’ report is being considered by my department.The recommendations will be fully considered in the context of the support services currently provided to children and young people with vision impairment through the Education Authority’s (EA) Sensory Service.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how he will ensure the £13 million funding on learning disability services is meaningfully co-designed with parents.
answer_text: My Department recognises the importance of co-design and the value it adds. The Framework for Children with Disabilities has been developed through meaningful engagement with families and providers to set the priorities for my Department and Trusts. It is recognised that further work is needed to better involve those that use our services and those that deliver our services. Meaningful co-design requires capacity and relationship building with families. My officials have committed to using existing engagement structures to identify and utilise opportunities to ensure experts by experience and providers can shape the services that will be expanded, however, as this process takes time, it will be challenging to ensure that co-design is meaningfully built into the recent allocation of additional funds, balanced against the need to produce urgent improvements to services. To guide the wider implementation of the Framework for Children with Disabilities, my officials intend to hold a workshop with the sector and service users in Spring 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when he will publish the review for eligibility for Free School Meals and school uniform grant.
answer_text: A public consultation on the review of the eligibility criteria for free school meals and uniform grants was launched on Friday 22 November 2024. The consultation will run for 12 weeks until Friday 14 February 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when he will make  a decision on the development proposal for Spires Integrated Primary School.
answer_text: Development Proposal (DP) 672 which proposes that a statutory nursery unit will be established at Spires Integrated Primary School to provide 26 part-time nursery places with effect from 1 September 2022, or as soon as possible thereafter, was published by the Education Authority on 20 January 2022. The two-month statutory objection period in relation to this DP ended on 21 March 2022 and my officials have prepared a report which has been passed to me to inform my decision.The assessment of any DP is a complex process and requires careful consideration of all evidence to ensure decisions are taken in the best interests of all children and to ensure they have access to high quality education that is viable and sustainable in the long term.I am aware of the need for clarity and certainty for the school and its community, therefore I will endeavour to provide that clarity and certainty as soon as practically possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on his Department's engagement with Ards and North Down Borough Council on how repairs to the floor of Portaferry Sports Centre, operating out of St Columba's College, will be progressed.
answer_text: A stakeholder meeting involving St Columba’s College, the school trustee, Ards and North Down Borough Council and the Department of Education was held on 8 November 2024. At that meeting, it was agreed that the school should submit an unavoidable minor works application to the Department, to enable the required works to the sports centre flooring to be scoped and costed for delivery. The application was subsequently received from St Columba’s College on 11 November 2024 and has been issued to the Education Authority for scoping. Subject to the necessary business case approvals, the project will be considered for delivery alongside other competing priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when he will make a decision on the development proposal for Magherafelt Nursery School.
answer_text: Development Proposal (DP) 704 which proposes that Magherafelt Nursery School will transform to Controlled Integrated status with effect from 1 September 2023, or as soon as possible thereafter, was published by the Education Authority on 7 September 2023. The two-month statutory objection period in relation to this DP ended on 14 November 2023 and my officials have prepared a report which has been passed to me to inform my decision.The assessment of any DP is a complex process and requires careful consideration of all evidence to ensure decisions are taken in the best interests of all children and to ensure they have access to high quality education that is viable and sustainable in the long term.I am aware of the need for clarity and certainty for the school and its community, therefore I will endeavour to provide that clarity and certainty as soon as practically possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for his assessment of the need for the Executive to ring-fence funding for agriculture, agri-environment, fisheries and rural development.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline the changes they have identified for a future good relations strategy.
To ask the Minister of Education whether his Department will create a register for non-teaching staff, similar to the Northern Ireland Supply Teacher Register.
answer_text: A bid for funding to enable the development of a register for non-teaching staff, equivalent to the Northern Ireland Supply Teacher Register (NISTR), is one of a number of proposals for digital transformation currently being considered by the interim Public Sector Transformation Board.  The proposal is to establish a digital system to enable schools and the Education Authority to search for support staff available in a particular area and engage them in work. To allow for a strategic, system-wide approach to digital transformation, the interim Public Sector Transformation Board has recommended that a digital landscape review be undertaken to facilitate collective consideration of all such bids.  This work is expected to be finalised by the end of this year and will enable further progress to be made on the digital proposals received.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when the updated Integrated Education Strategy and Action Plan will be published.
answer_text: It is anticipated that the updated Integrated Education Strategy and Action Plan will be published by the end of January 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the stakeholders from the health sector that will be invited to contribute to the Task and Finish Group on Reduction and Management of Restrictive Practices in Educational Settings.
answer_text: In finalising a list of organisations invited to join the Reduction and Management of Restrictive Practices in Educational Settings Task and Finish Group, consideration was given to those who expressed an interest in participating in the Group, alongside suggestions from a number of other stakeholders. The following stakeholders have been invited to join the Task and Finish Group:Public Health Agency Royal College of Nursing Northern Ireland Teaching CouncilNorthern Ireland Commissioner for Children and Young People Children’s Law CentreEducation Authority Representatives of Post Primary Mainstream, Primary Mainstream, Special Schools and EOTAS

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the actions taken in response to thematic review of physical education in primary schools, published in October 2022.
answer_text: My Department established a Task and Finish group to address the recommendations from the Education and Training Inspectorate's (ETI) review of physical education (PE) in primary schools. The group is made up of representatives from the Department, the Education and Training Inspectorate, The Council for the Curriculum, Examinations and Assessment and the Education Authority (EA).Work streams have been established, looking at Initial Teacher Education and professional development opportunities, the school estate, resources, and providing support to schools to help them plan and deliver the recommended two hours of curricular PE per week. A circular is being drafted currently which will provide practical guidance to schools on how to address the recommendations of the report.Additionally, my Department is also working with the EA and teaching practitioners to create exemplars of good practice for schools and teachers seeking to enhance their curricular PE provision.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the capital funding available to schools moving to full time pre-school offer as part of standardisation of Pre-School Education Programme.
answer_text: It is my intention that approximately 100 pre-school settings will transition to offer 22.5 hours pre-school education provision per week from September 2025. My Department has published for consultation three tranches consisting of a total of 107 pre-school settings that are being considered for a transition to the standardised session length from September 2025.  None of these settings require capital investment to deliver this.Capital investment will be required in future years as the Standardisation Programme continues to roll out.  The Scoping Study published on my Department’s website contains details of the resource and capital funding likely to be required for this.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether money allocated to schools who are establishing a Specialist Provision in a Mainstream School is specifically earmarked for that purpose or do schools have discretion on how to spend this funding.
answer_text: None of the funding allocated to schools for specialist provision classes is earmarked.Teaching and non-teaching staff costs are paid from the Education Authority’s (EA) central budget.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any advice he has received from officials in relation to meeting the Loyalist Communities Council.
answer_text: My officials routinely provide advice to me in relation to invitations from a range of schools, organisations and individuals. As Minister of Education, I may choose to accept that advice or ignore it. On this occasion, I felt it was important to hear the views of the Loyalist Communities Council on educational underachievement I am content that that was the right decision.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he undertook an assessment of the extent to which the Loyalist Communities Council represents the views of proscribed organisations in advance of him accepting their invitation to meet.
answer_text: I accepted the invitation to meet the Loyalist Communities Council because they wished to discuss educational underachievement in loyalist communities. This is a subject of interest to any Minister of Education and was highlighted in "New Decade New Approach" and reflected in the report "A Fair Start". I will continue to work in the best interests of children and young people in everything I do.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when payments arising from the pay and grading review for non-teaching staff will be made.
answer_text: Due to affordability considerations, the Pay & Grading agreement will be implemented in two stages. Stage 1 will move eligible staff onto their new pay scales with effect from 1 April 2024.  The Education Authority have advised these payments will be made in November 2024 as part of the normal pay process.  Payments for Stage 2 of the agreement will be made in the next financial year, in line with the agreed settlement.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how he will ensure there are pathways available for all young people to facilitate education to age 18.
answer_text: In line with the Independent Review of Education recommendation, and subject to Executive agreement, I intend to legislate to ensure that all children remain in education or training until they are 18 years old.To ensure appropriate pathways for all young people there will be engagement with a range of relevant stakeholders on the further development of proposals and given the links with Further and High Education, this will include the Minister for the Economy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the funding provided to special schools to facilitate extra-curricular activities.
answer_text: Under the Extended Schools programme, my department provided funding in 2024/25 of over £599k to 39 special schools to facilitate the provision of a range of services including (but not exclusive to) breakfast clubs, before and after school activities, literacy and numeracy support, parental engagement.The Education Authority (EA) provides funding to support summer provision for special schools to 21 of the 39 schools via their block grant.  An additional £655k was allocated in 2024/25 to enhance current provision and support additional provision across the remaining 18 special schools. Programmes associated with special schools have been considered as part of the End to End (E2E) Review of Special Educational Needs (SEN) Special Schools workstream, the findings of which have been used to shape and inform a draft SEN Reform Agenda and associated delivery plan for transformation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any engagement that has taken place with the Northern Ireland Training Council in relation to teacher workload.
answer_text: In April 2024 the Workload Agreement, TNC 2024/2, was jointly approved and issued with the Northern Ireland Teachers Council (NITC). Updated online training for school Principals, Boards of Governors and Teachers covering directed time budgets and the Workload Agreement was devised and issued by the Education Authority.A framework to address the recommendations contained in the 8 completed workforce reviews was jointly devised with NITC. The recommendations from the 8 completed workforce reviews have been jointly assessed with NITC and categorised into a framework.The joint framework identifies those recommendations which; have been completed and are now in operation in the intervening period since the reviews were carried out and agreement was reached,are in progress with identified lead individuals and expected completion dates,can now be progressed within existing resource in the immediate to short term, are being addressed through other programmes of work including the DE end to end reviews of school improvement and SEN but are outside of the remit of TNC. A timetable for the implementation of those areas which can be progressed, both immediately and over a short to medium term has now been agreed with NITC. The focus by both Management Side and the NITC is to determine the areas of work which, once revised and implemented, will have the potential to impact positively on working practices within the education sector.A programme of work has been jointly agreed with NITC.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether the (i) Belfast Wide Early Years will be funded to end of academic year;  and (ii) pilot will be funded beyond the academic year, to ensure data collection is effective.
answer_text: I would refer the Member to the answer to question AQW 17240/22-27.    The answer is contained in the Written Answers Booklet for week ending 8 November 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the scope of the Review of the Northern Ireland Curriculum.
answer_text: The review will make a series of policy recommendations regarding the purpose, design, specification and implementation of the Northern Ireland Curriculum. It is also expected to provide a strategic assessment of the current curriculum within the international context.  The review will:identify emerging curricular themes and key issues of public/political/media interest; consider the role of curriculum design in tackling educational disadvantage and reducing educational inequalities;produce coherent proposals and recommendations to support the future development/reform of the curriculum; andmake recommendations on how the curriculum should be monitored, reviewed and developed with relevant experts in the field, key education organisations, schools and other key stakeholders.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether the need for intergenerational learning will be reflected in the Review of the Northern Ireland Curriculum.
answer_text: The review of the Northern Ireland curriculum, to be undertaken by Lucy Crehan, will make a series of policy recommendations regarding the purpose, design, specification and implementation of the Northern Ireland Curriculum.  The review will commence shortly; therefore, it is too soon to comment on priority areas of inquiry.  However, it is unlikely that the final report will include detailed proposals on individual course/subject content.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail what engagement has been undertaken regarding the 2024/2025 pay claim for teachers.
answer_text: Through the Teachers’ Negotiating Committee (TNC) a joint pay group is operational and meetings have taken place with employers, sectoral support bodies and the recognised teaching trade unions (NITC) to explain and update them on the current financial pressures in Education and the current pay claim from NITC. The NITC pay claim is substantially higher than what has been recommended by the UK Government and accepted by teachers in England.  In line with the requirements of Public Sector Pay Policy, any proposals for a teachers’ pay award must be affordable and this is proving difficult to resolve given the pressures that already face the education budget. The Chair of TNC Management Side has also written to NITC formally requesting for a de-escalation and for the formal ballots to be stood down.I will continue to do all I can to secure funding for an appropriate pay settlement for teachers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer (i) AQW 15354/22-27; AQW 15914/22-27; and AQW 15980/22-27.

To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 16286/22-27, (i) whether any final approval is required from his Department to release the Entitlement Framework funding due to Strangford Integrated College, which was withheld in error by the Education Authority; and (ii) when the payment will be made to Strangford Integrated College.
answer_text: My Department has given the necessary approval to the Education Authority to make the payment to Strangford Integrated College.  The EA has confirmed that the payment to Strangford Integrated College will be made on 14th November 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline the support provided by his Department to pre-school and nursery school settings with Children Looked After on their enrolment.
answer_text: My department does not currently provide additional supports to pre-school and nursery school settings with Children Looked After. Interventions have been developed and piloted for children looked after as part of the Early Intervention Transformation Programme, however the initial focus has been on primary-age children.  Any expansion into early years settings will be contingent upon the availability of financial resources.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline what steps they have taken to define sectarianism.
answer_text: We can all agree that there is no room in our society for sectarianism, or intolerance, in any shape or form.Our ongoing commitment to eradicating sectarianism is reflected through the Together Building a United Community Strategy. The T:BUC strategy includes a working definition of sectarianism where it is defined as: ‘threatening, abusive or insulting behaviour or attitudes towards a person by reason of that person’s religious belief or political opinion; or to an individual as a member of such a group.’	Going forward, the issue of sectarianism and how we can continue to tackle it in the context of today’s society is under consideration as part of the T:BUC Review, and it is expected that recommendations will shortly be submitted for Ministerial consideration.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) for an update on the future of the Belfast Inclusion Health Service following the closure of their site on Townsend Street; and (ii) to detail any impact this will have on healthcare provision for people experiencing homelessness.
answer_text: On the 30 July 2024, the Belfast Inclusion Health Service (BIHS) had to vacate their premises in Townsend Street due to an attack on the Welcome Organisation premises. The service has been temporarily located within the Shankhill Wellbeing & Treatment Centre and have reconfigured their service delivery to ensure the limited downturn in services provided, where possible for this population.The GP sessions have also been relocated to Crumlin Road Health Centre.Given this relocation, unfortunately the drop-in service is currently not operational. The impact on access to healthcare for this population continues to be met, where possible, across existing services.Relocation options continue to be explored, to support the re-introduction of the Drop-In service and the co-location of all key stakeholders in the provision of this service.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when the modular classroom required for Camphill Primary School's Nurture Room, originally approved from September 2022, will be provided on-site at the school.
answer_text: My Department has allocated additional funding to the Education Authority (EA) to enable Camphill Primary School’s Nurture Room provision to be progressed. I understand that the EA will move to award the contract as soon as possible and once the contractor is appointed the EA project officer will be in contact with the school to advise of the programme of the works and confirm when the provision will be in place.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure why Translink is not providing additional bus services from Strangford to Downpatrick during the suspension of the Strangford Lough ferry service.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and can confirm that Translink engaged with stakeholders regarding the passenger-only operation on the Strangford Ferry service from 14th October 2024 to 18th November 2024.  School pupils were advised to avail of the ferry service to connect with their bus in Strangford.  In addition, Translink are providing an extra vehicle for the provision of school transport, from Portaferry to Assumption Grammar School.  For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department will reinstate an evening service for the Strangford foot passenger ferry schedule.
answer_text: My Department was granted approval by MCA and HSENI to operate this reduced evening service during the suspension period. The normal sailing schedule will resume when the works have been completed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure why an evening ferry service has not been included in the foot passenger Strangford ferry schedule.
answer_text: My Department procured a passenger only vessel to maintain an essential link between Portaferry and Strangford during the essential slipway works. With a limited number of suitable boat operators available and complying with MCA and HSENI requirements the safest possible solution has been provided.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when teachers who retired at the end of 2023-24 academic year will receive their pension and lump sum payment.
answer_text: The Department of Education received 558 pension applications relating to teachers retiring in August 2024.  Pensions are in payment and lump sum payments have been made for 455 of these applicants.A further 87 applicants have been provided with details of their option choice for retirement.  When these applicants return their choices, lump sum payments will normally credit to their bank account within six working days of receipt, and they will be added to the next monthly payroll due for payment.The remaining 16 cases being processed mainly relate to applications received after the deadline for September payments.  Option choices for applications received by end July 2024 will be issued to the applicants by 31 October 2024 in line with normal teachers’ pension timescales.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 15278/22-27, to detail (i) the number of applications received for the most recent tranche of the Fair Play Grant; and (ii) the number that were awarded a grant.
answer_text: (i) the number of applications received for the most recent tranche of the Fair Play Grant; The Department of Health, through the Childcare Partnership, facilitates an annual grant scheme to ensure children with additional needs are included in early years services, known as Fair Play. The funding provides support measures with a focus on the individual needs of children who are accessing quality play and learning experiences in registered community, voluntary and private provision throughout the academic year. These support measures can and do include additional staffing capacity.The Childcare Partnership also provides free training for early years practitioners in a range of subjects to do with additional needs in tandem with this financial support. In the 2024/25 tranche 154 registered early years settings applied. (ii) the number that were awarded a grant.        The most recent figures indicate the number of applicant groups that benefitted from an award was 89 which included 343 children in their care.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any information that has been shared with impacted schools detailing the changes to ferry and bus services for pupils using the replacement Strangford passenger ferry.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) is responsible for the operation and delivery of home to school transport. I am advised that the EA has worked alongside colleagues from Translink and the Department for Infrastructure (DfI) Ferry Service to facilitate alternative arrangements for pupils who would normally avail of the ferry service to complete their home to school journey.I understand that it was agreed that as the majority of queries were relating to the ferry service itself, the DfI Ferry service would lead on sharing information with schools. Initial contact was made with schools in mid-September to brief them on the plans for the works to be carried out at the ferry terminal and the proposed mitigations being considered. A further updated issued in early October and information was made available on the DfI website on 7th October and on NI Direct to confirm that Assumption Grammar School pupils would be provided with a substitute bus service and a passenger-only ferry would be in operation for pupils from other schools.Due to the various safety steps required ahead of introducing the replacement passenger ferry which needed to be confirmed with the Health and Safety Executive Northern Ireland and the Maritime & Coastguard Agency, final arrangements with regards to the timings of the replacement ferry service were not confirmed for the EA to publish until Friday 11 October. These details were shared online by DfI on the Strangford Ferry information pages, confirmed with the schools and were also published on the EA website and through its social media channels. Further communication was issued via the DfI Strangford Ferry Service text messaging service. EA Transport ensured that a representative was on site on the first day of the alternative arrangements on Monday 14 October, ahead of the first pupils arriving at 7.30am.  Representatives from Assumption Grammar School were also in attendance on Monday.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he plans to establish a capital fund for Irish-medium schools.
answer_text: My department is mindful of its statutory duty to encourage and facilitate the development of Irish-medium education and works closely with the sector to identify emerging capital priorities. Over the last ten years, the Department has invested significantly across Irish-medium schools, with announced major works projects totalling over £60.4million. Over £25million has also been invested in minor capital works, ranging in size and scale across the sector.An Accommodation Fund was established in 2010 with a grant of £2million to address accommodation deficiencies in the Irish-medium sector. This fund has been replenished on a number of occasions and its scope has been expanded to include enabling works, health & safety works and land purchases. The Department is currently working with the sector on options for a number of Irish-medium schools that have outgrown their existing sites.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when he will formally respond to the departmentally-funded Fair? Shared? Supported? research report.
answer_text: My department is committed to engaging further with Comhairle na Gaelscolaíochta (CnaG) to discuss the concerns regarding the shortage of Irish-medium teachers highlighted in the report published by Queens University Belfast (‘Fair? Shared? Supported? Examining expectations and realities for Irish-medium practitioners’). We are in the process of examining their recommendations and engaging with CnaG on this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the plans in place to address the additional demand for parking in Portaferry and Strangford, given that passengers who would ordinarily have used their cars on the crossing will now be using the replacement foot passenger ferry.
answer_text: Anyone wishing to park and use the passenger ferry service from Portaferry will be directed to the free council car parks at Meetinghouse Street and The Rope Walk. Those travelling from Strangford can use the free car park adjacent to Stella Maris Street to the rear of the ferry terminal.If there are any concerns regarding motorists parking vehicles in such a manner that causes an obstruction, or blocking access or egress, then this should be referred to the PSNI.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when the Welcome Organisation will have new premises.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive and been advised that:“The process to secure new premises for the Welcome Organisation is ongoing and we are continuing to explore options. However, there are several factors which are taken into account in any relocation process. This includes planning, leases, cost, readiness of the premises for occupation, location, proximity to businesses and communities, etc. Therefore, it is not possible to provide a timeline for securing alternative premises.”	For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the NIHE directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the actions taken in response to the findings of the departmentally-commissioned assessors in relation to the governance of CCEA, appointed in October 2022 as referenced in FOI/2024-0087.
answer_text: The DE assessors made a number of recommendations related to the governance of the organisation, including the structure of CCEA Council, its committees and the need for a DE/CCEA Partnership Agreement. An action plan has been developed, informed by these recommendations which includes actions for DE and CCEA. As outlined in the response to FOI/2024-0087, implementation of this plan is ongoing and is largely exempt from disclosure under Section 35(1)(a) (the formulation or development of government policy) until all actions are complete.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether specific actions relating to Irish-medium settings will be included in the End to End Review of Special Educational Needs.
answer_text: The Education Authority’s (EA) Pupil Support Services (PSS) include the following special educational needs (SEN) support services which are available to all settings including those within the Irishmedium education (IME) sector:Autism Advisory and Intervention ServiceLiteracy ServiceSEN Inclusion ServiceLanguage & Communication ServiceSEN Early Years Inclusion ServiceSensory ServiceMy department is concluding its End to End (E2E) Review of SEN.  The specific needs relating to SEN provision in IME schools have been considered through engagement with Comhairle na Gaelscolaíochta (CnaG) and the IME sector.The emerging findings from the E2E Review of SEN regarding support services for the IME sector have been discussed with the EA and it has been agreed to carry out an audit of SEN provision in IME educational settings.Educational Psychology Services (EPS) are currently not delivered through the medium of Irish.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the specific services for special educational needs support provided by the Education Authority in Irish-medium settings.
answer_text: The Education Authority’s (EA) Pupil Support Services (PSS) include the following special educational needs (SEN) support services which are available to all settings including those within the Irishmedium education (IME) sector:Autism Advisory and Intervention ServiceLiteracy ServiceSEN Inclusion ServiceLanguage & Communication ServiceSEN Early Years Inclusion ServiceSensory ServiceMy department is concluding its End to End (E2E) Review of SEN.  The specific needs relating to SEN provision in IME schools have been considered through engagement with Comhairle na Gaelscolaíochta (CnaG) and the IME sector.The emerging findings from the E2E Review of SEN regarding support services for the IME sector have been discussed with the EA and it has been agreed to carry out an audit of SEN provision in IME educational settings.Educational Psychology Services (EPS) are currently not delivered through the medium of Irish.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the Educational Psychology Support that is provided in Irish to students in Irish-medium schools.
answer_text: The Education Authority’s (EA) Pupil Support Services (PSS) include the following special educational needs (SEN) support services which are available to all settings including those within the Irishmedium education (IME) sector:Autism Advisory and Intervention ServiceLiteracy ServiceSEN Inclusion ServiceLanguage & Communication ServiceSEN Early Years Inclusion ServiceSensory ServiceMy department is concluding its End to End (E2E) Review of SEN.  The specific needs relating to SEN provision in IME schools have been considered through engagement with Comhairle na Gaelscolaíochta (CnaG) and the IME sector.The emerging findings from the E2E Review of SEN regarding support services for the IME sector have been discussed with the EA and it has been agreed to carry out an audit of SEN provision in IME educational settings.Educational Psychology Services (EPS) are currently not delivered through the medium of Irish.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he has carried out an audit of the specific needs relating to special educational needs provision in Irish-medium schools.
answer_text: The Education Authority’s (EA) Pupil Support Services (PSS) include the following special educational needs (SEN) support services which are available to all settings including those within the Irishmedium education (IME) sector:Autism Advisory and Intervention ServiceLiteracy ServiceSEN Inclusion ServiceLanguage & Communication ServiceSEN Early Years Inclusion ServiceSensory ServiceMy department is concluding its End to End (E2E) Review of SEN.  The specific needs relating to SEN provision in IME schools have been considered through engagement with Comhairle na Gaelscolaíochta (CnaG) and the IME sector.The emerging findings from the E2E Review of SEN regarding support services for the IME sector have been discussed with the EA and it has been agreed to carry out an audit of SEN provision in IME educational settings.Educational Psychology Services (EPS) are currently not delivered through the medium of Irish.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) the schools in County Down  currently without school meals provision due to staff shortages; (ii) any contingency measures in place to ensure all pupils in these schools continue to have access to school meals; and (iii) when full school meal provision will be reinstated in these schools.
answer_text: There are currently no schools in County Down without school meals provision. Due to staffing issues, the Education Authority (EA) is currently only able to offer a packed lunch service to all pupils at Downshire Primary School. The EA has a number of contingencies in place when disruption to school meal provision occurs and these are considered in the following order: Meals can be transported from another school with a production kitchen if it has sufficient capacity;Prepared meals can brought into the school, providing there are sufficient staff and oven capacity available to heat/ serve the meals;Packed lunch service can be offered to all pupils, subject to the capacity of the external provider;Packed lunches can be provided for pupils entitled to free school meals only, subject to the capacity of the external provider.EA Catering will continue to prioritise the resumption of a normal meals service in Downshire Primary School as soon as staff are in position to ensure meals can be prepared and served in a safe environment, meeting food safety regulations. As this is dependent on staff returning to work and/or the completion of a recruitment process, it is not possible to provide a definitive date.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department is carrying out any analysis to ensure that there is consistency of provision for students with special educational needs and disabilities in all further education colleges.
answer_text: My Department currently provides £4.5 million per annum to the Further Education colleges to help provide the necessary additional technical and/or personal support required to allow students with special educational needs or disabilities to participate in mainstream further education courses, or in discrete programmes. Provision of this support is determined by Colleges.  It is based on the individual needs of the learner and is demand-led.My Department is currently undertaking a review of student support within further education, including provisions for those with special educational need and disabilities, to ensure that funding is being targeted equitably and at those most in need.  In addition, as you are aware, I have commissioned a review from officials on the existing skills and training provision for young people with special educational needs, including the legislative protections.  I want every young person to have the opportunity to access opportunities and fulfil their potential. The work is complex and involves many different stakeholders and issues so it will take time. My officials are currently working through the planning and scoping activity and liaising with a range of interested parties to get their insights and thoughts to help with shaping the work.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the resources provided to further education colleges to purchase additional equipment to meet the medical needs of students with special educational needs and disabilities.
answer_text: My Department provides £4.5 million per annum, ring-fenced funding through the Additional Support Fund to Further Education colleges to help provide the necessary additional technical and/or personal support required to allow students with special educational needs or disabilities to participate in mainstream Further Education courses, or in discrete programmes.		Examples of technical support include specialised enabling equipment, such as braillers, specialist software and hearing loops. Personal support includes specialist tutors/advisers, such as hearing or visual impairment tutors, interpreters, classroom assistants and note-takers. The level of support provided depends on both the nature of the need or disability and the course of study.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy who is responsible for providing personal care to students with special educational needs and disabilities who require this in further education colleges.
answer_text: The Health and Social Care Trusts under the leadership of the Department for Health, are responsible for providing arrangements to address personal care needs for people with disabilities, learning difficulties or significant health issues. Should a person in receipt of a personal care allowance, be it through Direct Payments or otherwise wish to attend a college course, the Further Education colleges will facilitate them in bringing their care service with them while they study.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether speech and language therapy can be provided in Irish in Irish-medium schools.
answer_text: Speech and Language Therapy (SLT) is not currently provided in Irish in Irish medium schools.Speech and Language Therapy can be provided, in collaboration with interpreting services, Irish medium school staff and parents, where required.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the training provided to further education college staff to meet the needs of students with special educational needs and disabilities.
answer_text: Each Further Education college provides a range of training and support for lecturing and support staff so that they can meet the needs of students with special educational needs and disabilities. Recent examples of training undertaken by college staff include examination access arrangements training, behavior management training and supporting learner resilience and wellbeing training. Where students have specific conditions that require tailored support, Further Education colleges provide bespoke training to staff to assist them in meeting this need, for example, supporting neurodiversity in education training is provided to staff who are delivering targeted support for neurodiverse learners.	This training ensures that Further Education college staff are appropriately equipped to provide the best possible educational provision to students with special educational needs and disabilities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when Entitlement Framework Funding due to Strangford Integrated College, which was withheld in error by the Education Authority, will be paid to the school.
answer_text: The EA has confirmed that the payment to Strangford Integrated College is currently being progressed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 15426/22-27, to detail the engagement undertaken with (i) schools; (ii) Translink; and (iii) the local community to develop local contingency plans prior to announcing the temporary suspension of the Strangford ferry service.
answer_text: My Officials have been engaged with the Education Authority and Translink to consider mitigation options which minimise impact on school pupils since August. Direct contact with Schools about suspension and mitigation measures continues.Officials regularly engage with elected representatives and community groups, the most recent meetings took place on 11 March and 30 September 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 15510/22-27, (i) whether the foot passenger ferry will be operational by 14 October 2024; and (ii) whether appropriate bus services for pedestrians and schoolchildren will be provided for their onward journeys.
answer_text: My Department has procured a passenger only service which subject to the necessary MCA certification and insurances being provided will be in operation from 14 October. A bus substitution will be provided by My Department via Translink from and to Portaferry for Assumption Grammar School pupils, other bus services will not be affected.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the Interim Public Service Transformation Board.
To ask the Minister of Education for his assessment of the need for a play strategy.
answer_text: Given the significant resource pressures we are facing, the development of a play strategy is not currently a priority for my department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the current budget allocated to meet the needs of children looked after in preschool settings.
answer_text: The Health and Social Care Trusts do not have a dedicated budget for looked after children in preschool settings.  The cost of addressing the needs of looked after children in pre-school settings is met from the Looked After Children budget allocated to HSC Trusts to enable them to fulfil their duties under The Children (NI) Order 1995 (the Order).  This includes the duties under Part IV of the Order as they relate to support for children and families.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 14900/22-27, whether the Inclusive Education Policy will consider aspects of inclusion in education in addition to special educational needs, including (i) ethnicity; (ii) socio-economic background; (iii) gender; (iv) sexuality; and (v) community background.
answer_text: The work undertaken by my officials, as part of early policy development, has highlighted that a central tenet to any inclusive education policy is that ‘all learners vulnerable to exclusion’ are considered.This includes those learners with characteristics protected under Section 75 of the Northern Ireland Act 1998 and enables a broader scope to be taken, recognising that children and young people can develop vulnerabilities, which require additional support, at any time.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether an analysis has been carried out of the budgetary savings for the Child and Adolescent Mental Health Service that could be realised by investing in primary school counselling.
answer_text: The case for investing in therapeutic interventions in schools is well made with recent studies on a UK basis showing the positive impact that they can have on both pupil wellbeing and prevention of long-term mental illness. I am unaware of any such specific studies which have taken place relating to provision of these services in Northern Ireland. However, early intervention and prevention remains a priority under the Mental Health Strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the work being undertaken between his Department, the Education Authority and the Department of Education to ensure the needs of children looked after are being met in preschool settings.
answer_text: My Department and the Department of Education and our respective arms-length bodies jointly own the Strategy for Looked After Children, A Life Deserved, which seeks to improve the wellbeing of looked after children, regardless of their age or where they receive services. Looked after children reviews, which include representation from education, consider the needs of all looked after children holistically and how these should be met. This includes a consideration of the child’s emotional and educational development and general wellbeing needs. Depending on the age of the child, this could include supporting access to pre-school or providing greater support at pre-school. Health and Social Care Trusts, in their role of early years services regulators, meet regularly with the Education Authority to consider pre-school placement provision for all children.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 15424/22-27 and AQW 15425/22-27, to detail the work being undertaken between his Department, the Education Authority and the Department of Health to ensure the needs of children looked after are being met in preschool settings.
answer_text: My officials work with their counterparts in the Department of Health (DoH) and the Education Authority (EA) to deliver the actions within the joint DE and DoH A Life Deserved: "Caring" for Children and Young People in Northern Ireland Strategy. The Strategy acknowledges that joint working is key, particularly between schools, the EA and Health and Social Care Trusts if we are to help Children Looked After (CLA) reach their full potential.  Most of the current work is focussed on the rollout of interventions developed and piloted for CLA under the Early Intervention and Transformation Programme.  This has taken place for primary-aged CLA only so far.  There is ongoing scoping work to identify issues to be considered in advance of the service being extended beyond primary schools, however a phased approach to implementation is required.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the budget currently allocated to meet the needs of children looked after in preschool settings.
answer_text: My department does not presently allocate funding or have a budget for children looked after in pre-school settings.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance when she will act on the 2010 Law Commission recommendation to require formal notice to be served on all joint tenants when one or more of the tenants intend to sever the tenancy.
answer_text: The Law Commission recommendation was one of many considered in the context of a review of land law in this jurisdiction and the report came down in balance in favour of modest reform to require notice to be given in such circumstances. The arguments are balanced, between the desirability of joint tenants being notified of severance, and individuals being able to freely dispose of their property in any manner they chose, which is a fundamental and enshrined human right. There has been only one concern raised with the Department about this issue since the 2010 report, and in the context of existing resources and competing legislative work, implementation of this recommendation is not an immediate priority for my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) the number of children with special educational needs currently without a school place for the 2024-25 academic year; and (ii) the number of children with special educational needs given a placement for September 2024 still awaiting full access to that place while capital works are ongoing.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has reported that as of 2 October, there are no children with a statement of special educational needs (SEN) who are currently without a confirmed school place.The EA has reported that the number of children with a statement of SEN who are still awaiting full on-site access to their school placement while capital works are ongoing is 139.Contingency arrangements are in place for all children impacted.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to provide details of the proposed schedule for the Strangford Lough foot passenger ferry.
answer_text: My Department has a procurement competition for the provision of a suitable passenger only service during the works, subject to public expenditure processes. If the procurement is successful a reduced timetable will be published.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure why a foot passenger ferry has not been provided for schoolchildren during the maintenance of the slipways of the Strangford Lough ferry service.
answer_text: My Department currently has a procurement competition for the provision of a suitable passenger only service during the works, subject to public expenditure processes.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he plans to provide an uplift in Pathway Funding to reflect rising costs and staff wage pressures.
answer_text: The Pathway Fund provides a contribution to a setting towards the overall cost of delivering an Early Years project.2024-25 represents year two of a two-year funding cycle.  Funding made available through the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy package, aimed at stabilising the Early Years sector, has enabled additional settings to receive Pathway funding in 2024-25.I have also asked my officials to explore the potential of providing an uplift to current Pathway awards in 2024-25 to further assist with stabilisation, in light of ongoing project cost increases.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 14805/22-27, for his assessment of the need to provide additional financial support to preschool and nursery settings with children looked after on their enrolment as is currently given to primary settings.
answer_text: My department is aware of the need to extend the support currently available to children looked after (CLA) in primary schools to those attending preschool and nursery settings, as well as those in post-primary school.  The current model of support for CLA delivered by the EA’s Primary Children Looked After Advisory Service (PCLAAS) was developed under the Early Intervention Transformation Programme. This model has been piloted for primary-aged children only at this stage. However, research indicates that CLA in nursery and preschool settings would also benefit from similar interventions and support. There are, however, operational factors that inhibit the potential extension of current provision.  These factors include the challenging funding context, as well as significant pressures within the health workforce.  My department continues to work closely with colleagues in DoH and the EA’s PCLAAS to improve outcomes for children looked after.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 14806/22-27, to detail the rationale for excluding nursery and preschool settings from support from the Education Authority Children Looked After Team.
answer_text: My department is aware of the need to extend the support currently available to children looked after (CLA) in primary schools to those attending preschool and nursery settings, as well as those in post-primary school.  The current model of support for CLA delivered by the EA’s Primary Children Looked After Advisory Service (PCLAAS) was developed under the Early Intervention Transformation Programme. This model has been piloted for primary-aged children only at this stage. However, research indicates that CLA in nursery and preschool settings would also benefit from similar interventions and support. There are, however, operational factors that inhibit the potential extension of current provision.  These factors include the challenging funding context, as well as significant pressures within the health workforce.  My department continues to work closely with colleagues in DoH and the EA’s PCLAAS to improve outcomes for children looked after.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any consultation his Department undertook with (i) schools; (ii) Translink; and (iii) the local community to develop contingency plans during the disruption of the Strangford ferry service.
answer_text: My Department issued a press release on 16 October out lining the intended arrangements for passengers during these essential works. Officials have direct contact with Schools, Translink the Education Authority and Local Residents Groups.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the actions his Department is taking to improve working conditions for staff in radiology.
answer_text: The Department regularly engages and supports collaboration across HSC Trusts on a wide range of workforce and people issues to ensure the delivery of safe, high quality services and to support Trusts in making HSC a rewarding and fulfilling place to work.HSC Trusts and other Arms Length Bodies are the employers of our HSC workforce and are responsible for maintaining and improving the working conditions of all staff working within HSC.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department will conduct a workforce review into diagnostic and interventional radiology.
answer_text: My Department is aware that the imaging workforce in Northern Ireland continues to be constrained by insufficient numbers. A costed workforce plan for imaging services, including diagnostic and interventional radiology, has been developed and outlines requirements to bring training within imaging teams up to date of approximately £6m per annum, over the next 3-5 years. Currently this remains unfunded.However, a business case is being developed for a multi-professional imaging training academy for Northern Ireland which will address radiology and advanced practice radiographer workforce deficit. The Imaging Academy intends to deliver an overall increase in imaging capacity within Health and Social Care for the benefit of all clinical specialties and support the reduction of excess waiting lists for diagnosis and treatment, as well as meeting the demands from unscheduled care.Progress remains subject to business case approval and availability of funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department provides additional financial support to nursery and preschool settings with children looked after on their enrolment.
answer_text: Health and Social Care Trusts have confirmed that they do not directly provide additional financial support to nursery and pre-school settings. However, HSC Trusts provide a range of additional supports to looked after children of pre-school age. Examples include a Sponsored Day Care Scheme in the Belfast Trust area, which enables Trust Children’s Community Services staff to secure day care provision to support families they are working with, including kinship carers.  This service is purchased at the rate set by providers and operates through a range of existing childcare providers across the Trust area.  The Southern Health and Social Care Trust funds day care placements for looked after children and the Northern Health and Social Care Trust also enables looked after children to access daycare/nursery support in its area.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on his proposed Dilapidation Bill.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when backdated payments, arising from the pay and grading resolution, will be made to non-teaching staff.
answer_text: I am pleased to confirm that the Education Authority (EA) are currently working on the system changes required to implement Stage 1 of the Pay & Grading agreement.  They are aiming to make these payments before the Christmas break.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 4168/22-27, to detail the specific engagement he has had with the Minister of Health in relation to appropriate mental health support for staff working in the homelessness sector.
answer_text: Engagement has taken place at Official, not Ministerial level.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the steps he is taking to reduce waiting times for cancer diagnostic tests.
answer_text: My Department has funded two Rapid Diagnosis Centres, delivering a pathway for patients with vague symptoms which could indicate cancer. Work is underway to develop new, site specific Rapid Diagnosis Pathways for patients with suspected cancer. On 25 September 2024, I announced that a regional breast cancer service would be established, with a regional waiting list for patients. This will enable women waiting for assessment to access the earliest appointment available across the region. Short term measures to address increasing wait times continue. This includes allocating additional funding to secure additional diagnostic capacity.  In 2023/24, £70.3m non-recurrent funding was allocated for patients requiring red flag and other time critical assessments and treatments. By March 2024, approximately 263,000 Outpatient/Advanced Healthcare Professional assessments, diagnostic tests and Inpatient/Day case procedures were delivered with an associated spend of approximately £70.3m£76m investment has been approved non-recurrently for 2024/25 for patients requiring red flag and other time critical assessments and treatments. As at the end of July 2024, approximately 75,000 Outpatient/Advanced Healthcare Professional assessments, diagnostic tests and Inpatient/Day case procedures have been delivered.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) for his assessment of the effectiveness of the Fair Play Grant Scheme as a reliable mechanism to support childcare providers meeting the needs of children with special educational needs and disabilities; and (ii) whether any alternative funding mechanisms are under consideration.
answer_text: (i) for his assessment of the effectiveness of the Fair Play Grant Scheme as a reliable mechanism to support childcare providers meeting the needs of children with special educational needs and disabilities;The Department of Health, through the Childcare Partnership, facilitates an annual grant scheme to ensure children with additional needs are included in early years services, known as Fair Play. The funding provides support measures with a focus on the individual needs of children who are accessing quality play and learning experiences in registered community, voluntary and private provision throughout the academic year. These support measure can and do include additional staff capacity.The Childcare Partnership also provides free training for early years practitioners in a range of subjects to do with additional needs in tandem with this financial support. In the 2024/25 financial year, the support to non-statutory early years providers by the Department of Health through the Fair Play Grant Scheme has been £425k. We believe the Fair Play Grant Scheme is reliable in that it is available recurrently each year. (ii) the forthcoming inter-departmental Early Learning and Childcare Strategy added additional resources to extend the reach of the Fair Play Grant Scheme in 2024/25 bringing the fund to a total of £675k in this financial year. This combined fund has been released to 83 early years and childcare providers benefitting 338 children.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the action his Department is taking to address the workforce shortfalls in (i) clinical radiologists; and (ii) clinical oncologists.
answer_text: Much has been achieved over recent years to grow the clinical radiologist workforce to meet increasing service demand.  Places on the local clinical radiology programme commissioned by my Department have been increased by 46%, from 37 (2015/16) to 54 (2022/23).  However, the 2024/25 Budget outcome was extremely disappointing for this Department meaning that a further three training places, which have been identified across the HSC Trusts, could not be recruited.  We will proceed to do so when the necessary funding is identified.  In addition, as identified in my Department’s Strategic Framework for Imaging Services in Health and Social Care (2018), a more innovative approach is required to enable further progress to be made in creating a local radiologist workforce of the size required to meet future demand.  Accordingly, a business case is being developed for a multi-professional Imaging Academy for Northern Ireland to address radiology and advanced practice radiographer workforce deficits.  Progress remains subject to business case approval and the availability of funding.Pending the establishment of the Imaging Academy, a costed workforce plan (approximately £6m per annum over the next 3-5 years) for imaging services has also been developed, to bring training within imaging teams, including clinical radiologists, up to date.  Currently this remains unfunded.The overall approach with respect to the clinical oncologist workforce is set out in A Cancer Strategy for Northern Ireland 2022 – 2032.  By 2024/25, £10.88m will have a been invested to stabilise HSC Trusts’ oncology and haematology services.  An additional 167.6 WTE posts have been provided across the full range of healthcare disciplines delivering oncology and haematology services as set out in the following table.Stabilisation of HSC Trusts’ Oncology and Haematology ServicesIncrease in WTE funded postsStaff typeOncologyHaematologyConsultant9.75 WTE7.58 WTESpecialty Doctor8.0 WTE8.0 WTEConsultant Radiographer/Specialist Radiographer6.5 WTERadiographer3.0 WTETOTAL27.25 WTE15.58 WTEGRAND TOTAL42.83 WTE

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the actions being taken to ensure that patients diagnosed with cancer receive their first treatment within 31 days.
answer_text: NI waiting time targets state that at least 98% of patients diagnosed with cancer should receive their first definitive treatment within 31 days of a decision to treat.  In the quarter ending March 2024, 2,949 patients started their first definitive treatment, 86.5% (2,551) of those patients started treatment within 31 days of a decision to treat, compared with 88.6% (2,463) in the previous quarter and 87.8% (2,445) in the same quarter last year. Increasing demand and workforce challenges are impacting on the 31-day standard.The Department continues to work with all Trusts to reduce the current waiting time for cancer treatments.  In the first two quarters of 2024/25 non-recurrent funding has been allocated to Trusts to support additional waiting list initiative work, using both inhouse and independent sector capacity.  In addition, the Department continues to facilitate the equalisation and reduction of waiting times through the inter-Trust transfer of patients. The Strategic Planning and Performance Group of DOH meet quarterly with trust colleagues to review performance. Trusts also provide Cancer Performance Optimisation Plans that are intended to reduce variation in practice, reduce variation in waiting times, maximising workforce capacity and skill mix, thereby optimising capacity and productivity within the Trust’s core service provision.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on initiatives to streamline and simplify the application process for free school meals.
answer_text: The EA continues to improve the free school meals (FSM) application process and additional measures implemented this year have further streamlined and simplified this process. These measures have seen a higher number of applications being submitted ahead of the start of the school year in 2024/25 than in 2023/24 and significantly improved EA processing times. This has enabled more pupils to access free school meals from the start of the academic year. The current processing time for FSM applications made to the EA is two working days, a significant improvement on the twelve day time at the same point in 2023.
