It will also take into account the statutory guidance on this issue that now applies in England and Wales.  Following the completion of initial engagement with key stakeholders and the development of draft guidance on the costs of uniforms, a public consultation will be carried out.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) how her Department monitors Boards of Governors’ adherence to departmental guidance on school uniform policy; and (ii) what actions her Department takes when guidance is not followed.
answer_text: The day-to-day management of schools, including the development and implementation of a school’s uniform policy, is a matter for each school Principal, subject to any directions that might be given by the Board of Governors. This allows schools the flexibility to set and apply their own uniform policy in a manner that can take account of the individual circumstances of their school and its pupils. My Department has issued non-statutory policy guidance (DE Circular 2011/04) to assist schools in relation to school uniform policy. As schools are not legally required to follow the guidance circular, my Department does not seek to monitor the extent of schools’ adherence to the advice within the guidance circular. However, my Department has re-issued the guidance circular to schools and I have made it clear to schools that every effort should be made to ensure school uniforms are as affordable as possible, in line with the Department’s guidance on this issue. You will also be aware of my announcement that I intend to put guidance on the costs of uniform on a statutory basis in order to make school uniforms more affordable for families. Should parents have concerns over the school uniform policy in their child’s school, they have the option to raise a complaint in line with the school’s  complaints procedure. If this procedure has been exhausted and the parent remains unsatisfied, they have right to refer the matter to the Northern Ireland Public Services Ombudsman.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any plans to establish a homeless unit within his Department.
answer_text: Homelessness is a cross-cutting issue and tackling it requires collaboration across Government.  The Department for Communities has policy responsibility to address homelessness, which is delivered via the Northern Ireland Housing Executive.Those who experience homelessness often have significant and complex health and social care needs.  Such is the expansive nature of these needs that the issue cuts across many different areas within the Department of Health (DoH), including Mental Health, Substance Use and Domestic and Sexual Violence.  The Mental Health Directorate within DoH currently holds policy responsibility for homelessness from a health perspective, and as such co-ordinates work across the relevant DoH policy areas.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) when is the current contract for carrying out Personal Independence Payments medical assessments due to end; and (ii) to detail her plans for delivering Personal Independence Payments medical assessments after this date.
answer_text: Health assessments for Personal Independence Payments (PIP) are carried out by Capita.  The current PIP contract is due to end on 31 July 2023. I have made clear that social security privatisation should be reversed and return to an in-housed health assessment service that puts peoples’ needs, equality and human rights at the heart of service provision.  The Department is working to progress this, however it will require Executive approval of the budget necessary to in-house key services and a collaborative partnership with the Department of Health.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what support his Department is providing to help Northern Ireland firms bid for Contracts for Difference, Allocation Round 5.
answer_text: The GB Contracts for Difference scheme does not extend to Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to (i) provide an update on the roll out of changes to the Common Selection Scheme; and (ii) detail when a review of the intimidation points system will take place.
answer_text: My Department is working with the Housing Executive to begin implementation of 18 of the 20 proposals set out in the December 2020 Consultation Outcome Report on “A Fundamental Review of Social Housing Allocations”. I have requested further work to be carried out and alternatives considered for Proposals 7 (the removal of Intimidation Points from the Housing Selection Scheme) and 9 (the removal of Interim Accommodation Points). Implementation will be carried out using a 3 phased approach. The Housing Executive is currently progressing a number of proposals within Phase 1 of the Review. Proposal 1 (an independent, tenure-neutral housing advice service) has been implemented. Proposals 5 (a greater choice of areas for all applicants for a social home), 15 (An applicant may receive two reasonable offers of accommodation) and 16 (social landlords may withdraw an offer of accommodation in specified circumstances) are in progress for completion by December 2022. The review of intimidation points is scheduled to complete in the third and final phase.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the protocol for counselors working on the Independent Counselling Service for Schools to refer students on to Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services.
answer_text: Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services (CAMHS) accept referrals from Educational Authority services, such as the Independent Counselling Service for Schools. As the Independent Counselling Service for Schools is within the remit of the Department of Education (DE), it would be more appropriate for DE to comment on the specific protocol in place for counsellors referring students into CAMHS.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the protocol for counsellors, working on the Independent Counselling Service for Schools, to refer students on to Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services.
answer_text: Child and Adolescent Mental Health Services (CAMHS) in Northern Ireland work within a stepped care model. The ICSS is identified as part of Step 2 of the CAMHS Stepped Care Model and is an early intervention approach.  When a school’s counsellor needs to make a referral to CAMHS they can make this referral directly subject to certain specified conditions and threshold criteria.Further information on the threshold criteria is detailed in the following link https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/camhs.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) when the current Independent Counselling Service for School contract expires; and (ii) when her Department will review and redesign the subsequent model for post-primary counselling.
answer_text: The current Independent Counselling Service for Schools (ICSS) contract expires on 31st August 2024. The Education Authority will commence a review of the current contract during the academic year 2022/2023 and based on this review and the needs of schools and young people, redesign the contract accordingly.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to provide an update on her Department’s consultation on a new policy to introduce an Intermediate Rent model in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Intermediate Rent intends to provide a new supply of more affordable housing for rent which offers good quality, more secure, below market rent homes for lower income households.The Department has consulted on outline proposals around the shape of an Intermediate Rent model here including detail on funding, delivery and tenancy arrangements, and published a Consultation Outcome Report in March 2022 setting out the feedback received, the Department’s response, and next steps to be taken.Work is ongoing to conclude the policy, legislative, and financial assistance model requirements to enable practical implementation. Completion of these remaining actions will support with the introduction of an Intermediate for Rent model here as soon as is practicable, and I intend to bring forward a final policy and financial assistance model upon completion of these identified actions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 1273/22-27, to provide details of all instances of displays of paramilitary flags on lamp posts which have been reported to the PSNI by his Department.
answer_text: In all instances, when a complaint is received about unauthorised flags flying from my Department’s equipment, officials will contact PSNI for advice.Between 1 January 2019 and 5 July 2022, my Department has requested advice from PSNI on 405 occasions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how many children, up to the age of 18 years, have Down Syndrome.
answer_text: I would refer the Member to AQW 1929/22-27

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) how many patients have completed the condition management programme for long COVID; and (ii) how many of these patients remain on waiting lists for other specialisms to assess their ongoing symptoms.
answer_text: Information on the number of people who have completed the condition management programme for long COVID is not available centrally in the Department.  The following information has been provided by the Health and Social Care (HSC) Trusts. how many patients have completed the condition management programme for long COVIDThe Post Covid Syndrome MDT (PCSS) clinic refers clients to the Condition Management Programme (CMP). They are two separate services.Belfast HSC Trust advised that they provide a bespoke pulmonary rehabilitation / dysfunctional breathing service for patients with significant respiratory symptoms post-COVID-19. 100 patients were referred to the service, who were assessed at a PCSS clinic.Northern HSC Trust advised that 25 patients were referred to the condition management programme who were assessed at a PCSS clinic.South Eastern HSC Trust advised that 329 patients have attended a PCSS clinic, which forms part of their condition management programme. Southern HSC Trust advised that 5 patients were referred to the condition management programme who were assessed at a PCSS clinic.Western HSC Trust advised that 24 patients were referred to the condition management programme who were assessed at a PCSS clinic.how many of these patients remain on waiting lists for other specialisms to assess their ongoing symptoms.Belfast, Southern and Western HSC Trusts advised that information for this response could only be obtained through a manual trawl through hundreds of files and could therefore only be supplied at disproportionate cost.Northern and South Eastern HSC Trust advised that 154 and 133, respectively, onward referrals have been made for assessment /intervention in other specialist areas but were unable to provide the waiting list situation for other services.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) any flood risk reports provided to the Department relating to the Glen River, at the culvert located near the junction of Belfast Road and Manse Road, Newtownards, in the last 10 years including allocated flood numbers; (ii) what flood remediation works have been carried out at this location; and (iii) what correspondence there has been from the Department to the Drainage Council in relation to flood risk.
answer_text: My Department has not been provided with any flood risk reports within the last 10 years in relation to the Glen River, in proximity to the junction of the Belfast Road and Manse Road in Newtownards.There are four watercourses, designated under the terms of the Drainage (NI) Oder 1973, located within this general location.  Whilst, my Department has not carried out any flood remediation works at this location it undertakes routine inspection and maintenance of these watercourses and their associated infrastructure.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to provide the outcome of any feasibility study carried out to scope the installation of a footpath at Whiterock Bay in the direction of Ballydorn Road toward Down Cruising Club.
answer_text: All requests for improvements to the road network, including the provision of footway schemes or upgrading works, are assessed in line with my Department’s current policies and guidance and all works are subject to prioritisation with all viable proposals competing for the finite amount of funding available.This location was previously assessed for the potential provision of a footway and it established that Whiterock Bay would require the acquisition of residential and agricultural land, removal of hedges and mature trees and the provision of accommodation works which would be significant in terms of scale and cost.  When assessed in line with my Department’s current policies and guidance, this scheme remains a low priority at this time against other competing schemes for the limited resources available.Should you have any further questions on this please contact DfI Roads Southern Division at dfiroads.southern@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how many patients are on a waiting list to access long COVID services.
answer_text: Information on the number of people who are on the waiting list for their first appointment at a Post COVID clinic is not available centrally in the Department.  The following information has been provided by the Health and Social Care Trusts.Western Health and Social Care Trust were contacted for input but were unable to provide a response within the required timeframe.Number of Patients Waiting for a first appointment at a Post-COVID clinicHSC TrustNumber of Patients WaitingBelfast 133Northern353South Eastern202Southern156Source: HSC Trusts

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what plans she has to review the Circular 2011/04 - Guidance to schools on school uniform policy, to address the costs of school uniforms for parents.
answer_text: Guidance on school uniforms was updated and issued to schools in 2018:https://www.education-ni.gov.uk/publications/circular-201104-guidance-schools-school-uniform-policy-revised-5-june-2018The Department commenced a Review of Free School Meals and Uniform Grant eligibility criteria in November 2021.  The purpose of this review is to ensure that FSM and uniform grants are available to support the most vulnerable children in our society and the review will consider the current rates of uniform grant as a contribution to the cost of school uniforms. The review will consider options to extend the FSM and uniform grant criteria and the rate of uniform grant and will consider putting the guidance onto a legislative footing.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) what analysis has been carried out to assess the benefits of the Engage II Programme in terms of learning outcomes; and (ii) what programme will follow on from Engage II in 2022/23.
answer_text: The Education and Training Inspectorate (ETI) published an evaluation report on 21 December 2021 evaluating the Engage Programme in primary and post-primary schools during the 2020/21 academic year.  A copy of the report is available on my Department’s website: www.etini.gov.uk/publications/evaluation-department-educations-engage-programme-primary-and-post-primary-schools.  The report detailed how schools provided effective intervention programmes for pupils whose learning was impacted adversely by the COVID-19 pandemic. A further report is being developed by the ETI for the second year of the Engage programme. Engage has ensured that our children and young people have been provided with a range of support to reduce the impact which the Covid-19 pandemic has had on their educational experience and learning.As regards a third year of the programme in the 2022/23 academic year, I have issued a Direction to my officials for the Engage programme to continue to December 2022.  The Department of Finance approved that Direction on 6 July 2022, and I am delighted that I have been able to allocate £10million to continue the programme until the end of December 2022.   I have recently written to schools and settings to inform them.It is my intention that the programme will continue until the end of the academic year. However, as this remains subject to the necessary funding being made available, settings are not able to commit to expenditure beyond December 2022. I will update schools on the budget position for the rest of the academic year as soon as I am in a position to do so.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what action his Department is taking to address paramilitary flags attached to lamp posts.
answer_text: The display of a flag of a proscribed terrorist organisation is a criminal offence that may be capable of investigation by the PSNI, and I would encourage anyone with information in regards the display of such items to contact the PSNI.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 154/22-27, when the new engagement forum to assist with the development of the Executive Childcare Strategy will be established
answer_text: My Department is currently developing plans for the establishment of a childcare stakeholder engagement forum, including membership and the Terms of Reference for the Group.  My officials intend to contact sectoral representatives over the summer months, with a view to convening the first meeting of the group shortly thereafter.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) how many children are assessed as living in poverty; and ii) how many of these children are eligible for free school meals.
answer_text: Official measures of absolute and relative poverty are derived from the Family Resources Survey (FRS). Both measures can be presented on a before and after housing costs basis. The number of children estimated to be living in poverty in 2019/20 are presented in the table below. Child Poverty Type2019/20Relative Poverty Before Housing Costs100,000Relative Poverty After Housing Costs103,000Absolute Poverty Before Housing Costs75,000Absolute Poverty After Housing Costs82,000Data collection in 2020/21 was impacted by COVID-19 restrictions resulting in a heavily reduced sample size. This has led to additional uncertainty around the estimates, particularly for smaller groups. As such, for 2020/21, only figures relating to the whole population  were published.Further information regarding the Family Resources Survey and related publications can be found at the link below.https://www.communities-ni.gov.uk/topics/family-resources-surveyIt is not possible to determine how many of these children are eligible for free school meals. It should be noted that free school meals fall under the remit of the Department of Education (DE).

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on (i) her Department's review into the School Uniform Grant and Free School Meals schemes; and (ii) the work of the stakeholder forum for this review, including details of when it has met.
answer_text: The Department has commenced a Review of Free School Meals and Uniform Grant eligibility criteria.  The review is considering a number of options for free school meals and uniform grants, including the options for universal free school meal provision for certain year groups or all pupils and the removal/adjustment to current thresholds for Universal Credit/Working Tax Credit as well as the potential to legislate on uniform policy.   My Department continues to work to develop costed options and a consultation exercise will take place later in the year.  In the interim period, I have agreed an increase of 20% to the existing uniform grant allowances for the forthcoming academic year.The stakeholder group met in December 2021 and another meeting will take place in the near future. The Department has also met with a cross departmental group and internal departmental group as well as the Northern Ireland Commissioner for Children and Young People. The input of key stakeholders and children and young people will be very important to ensure that the views of all interested parties are considered.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the disposal of land at Talbot Street, Newtownards, including what consideration has been given to a Community Asset Transfer being undertaken.
answer_text: My Department is disposing of two plots of land at Talbot Street in Newtownards. The Government Disposal Procedures provide a framework to facilitate community ownership of surplus public land as an option within the normal disposal process, known as Community Asset Transfer (CAT). Officials from my Department are engaging with a Third Sector Organisation to establishing whether disposal of one of the plots of land is feasible under CAT.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on (i) the research project commissioned to inform the development of a Childcare Strategy; and (ii) the current timeline for publication of the Childcare Strategy consultation.
answer_text: The COVID-19 Childcare Reference Group (CRG) played an important role in supporting the work of both the Department of Education and the Department of Health (DoH) during the emergency response to the pandemic.   However, now that my Department is re-focusing its efforts on the development of the Executive Childcare Strategy, a new engagement forum is needed to reflect this wider remit.   Many of the same organisations and interests will be represented on this new forum as were on the CRG, but it is important that we take this opportunity to refresh the membership and bring new voices to the table.  My Department has also launched the tender exercise to commission an independent research report on a Review of Childcare Services in Northern Ireland.  It is envisaged that a contract to take forward the review will be in place by end of June 2022 and will run to December 2022.  A separate exercise to produce a costed implementation plan for the standardisation of pre-school hours is also under consideration. 	Initial engagement with key stakeholders through a Strategic Insight Programme took place between October and December 2021 and further focused discussions on key themes will be required.   I am committed to progressing the Executive Childcare Strategy as quickly as possible, however we need the evidence from these substantial pieces of work in order to develop costed solutions for Executive consideration.
